社殿.jpg|サムネイル|本殿を覆う[[覆堂|覆屋]]]] 言い伝えによると、洩矢社は元々は天竜川のほとりにあった。境内にある藤の木が対岸の藤島神社の藤の木と絡み合い、大きい橋に見えるほど繁茂していた。[[寛文]]年間（1661年～1673年）の頃、[[諏訪藩|諏訪藩主]]は天竜川の蛍狩り遊覧のため藤を伐り払うように命じたが、人々は神の祟りを恐れて伐る者がいなかった。新屋敷の小石嘉右衛門という傲慢な人が山役の料（[[蔵米]]三[[升]]）二人分を条件に藤を伐採したところ、間もなく気がおかしくなってしまった。京都の[[吉田家]]に祈祷してもらい少し収まったが、ある日、祈祷の札を頭にのせて家を飛び出し、「半の木」という山腰（現・岡谷市半ノ木か）で突然倒れて死んだ。神罰を恐れた人々はその場所に神札を祀って「鎮目大明神」と名づけた。 命令を下した藩主にも祟りがあったため、お詫びに[[高島城|城内]]で祠を作らせて奉納することにしたが、あまりにも大きすぎて大手門を出ることができなかった。やむなく少し縮めて奉納されたのが現在の本殿であるといわれている。この際に当社を現在地へ遷座し、藩主が神事免を寄付した。それ以降は当社が諏訪藩主に篤く崇敬され、[[明治維新]]期の[[廃藩置県]]まで例祭は藩主によって行われた{{sfn|山本ひろ子|2016|p=213-212}}。 ==神徳== 霊験あらたかな神とされ、特に産婦が[[柄杓#柄杓に関する事柄|底抜けの柄杓]]を献納すれば安産ができるといわれている。 ==祭事== *[[元始祭]] *[[虫送り|除蝗祭]] *'''例祭'''（[[10月20日]]洩矢神社由緒書き看板。、現在は10月第3日曜日） *[[二年参り]]（12月31日） *'''[[御柱祭#小宮祭|御柱祭]]'''（寅年・申年） *:古来は諏訪大社下社の御柱祭と同日に行われたが、近年では諸事情により他の日で行なわれている。 なお、諏訪大社との深い関わりから、大社の[[幟|織旗]]と同じ太さのものを許されている。 ==交通アクセス== *[[東日本旅客鉄道|JR東日本]][[中央本線]] [[岡谷駅]]より徒歩約15分（約1.1km） * [[シルキーバス]]川岸橋原線 「洩矢社前」バス停より徒歩1分 ==関連場所== ===洩矢大神御旧趾碑=== [[ファイル:Moriya-no-Ōkami Gokyūseki - 洩矢大神御舊趾.jpg|サムネイル|200px|洩矢大神御舊趾]] 当社から約300m（徒歩5分）離れた位置にある石碑。碑銘は神長官・守矢実顕（第75代、1825年～1903年）の揮毫によるもの。遷座記念碑によると元々は「洩矢大神の住居跡」（上述の経塚遺跡）にあったが、昭和53年（1978年）に[[中央自動車道]]の建設事業により現在地に移された。 '''位置：''' {{coord|36|02|56|N|138|02|05|E|region:JP}} ===龍源山の神=== 洩矢神社から約1.3km（徒歩19分）離れた岡谷市湊（旧花岡村）にある[[祠]]であり、昔は「'''左山神'''（ひだりやまのかみ）」と呼ばれていた。祭神は[[オオヤマツミ|大山祇神]]。 当地の伝承によれば、かつては洩矢神がこの周辺にあった部落の人々に[[産土神]]として崇められていた。二十数軒もある部落であったが、立地条件に恵まれていないため、[[明暦]]（1655年～1658年）の頃から人々が段々とここを離れ、他所へ移動していた。花岡姓を名乗る一軒が隣の橋原村へ引っ越した際に、ここに祀られていた洩矢神を橋原のほうに遷座し、その社の跡に[[山の神]]を祀って松の木を植えた。木の枝が栄えたため、「{{読み仮名|枝垂|しだ}}れ山の神」（後に「左山の神」に転化）と呼ばれるようになったという。 中央道の通過により、その工事用道路として拡幅があり、旧地から一段上へ移転された。 '''位置：'''{{coord|36|03|09|N|138|02|53|E|region:JP}} [[File:Ryūgen Yama-no-Kami - 龍源山の神.jpg|サムネイル|左|140px|龍源山の神]] ==関連項目== *[[諏訪大社]] *[[守矢氏]] *[[守屋山]] *[[藤島神社 (諏訪地方)]] *[[小野神社・矢彦神社]] - 洩矢神と戦う前に建御名方神がしばらく滞在したという伝承を持つ神社 *[[東方風神録 〜 Mountain of Faith.]] - ゲーム内に洩矢神に由来する登場人物があるため、そのファンの「[[巡礼 (通俗)|聖地]]」となっている{{cite web|url=https://www.asahi.com/articles/ASKB66VTHKB6UOOB00T.html|title=ゲーム人気で聖地に　岡谷の洩矢神社|publisher=[[朝日新聞]]|date=2017-10-28|accessdate=2018-10-03}}{{cite web|url=http://www.shimin.co.jp/2017/12/%E3%82%B2%E3%83%BC%E3%83%A0%E5%88%87%E3%81%A3%E6%8E%9B%E3%81%91%E9%96%A2%E5%BF%83%E6%9B%B4%E3%81%AB%e3%80%80%E6%A9%8B%E5%8E%9F%E6%B4%A9%E7%9F%A2%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E3%81%AB%E5%A4%9A%E3%81%8F%E3%81%AE/|title=ゲーム切っ掛け関心更に　橋原洩矢神社に多くのファン|website=信州・市民新聞グループ|date=2017-12-01|publisher=株式会社岡谷市民新聞社|accessdate=2018-10-03}}{{cite web|url=https://moriyajinja.amebaownd.com/posts/1644415|title=東方projectの聖地巡礼らしいです|website=洩矢神社公式HP|date=2016-11-22|accessdate=2018-10-03}} *[[岡谷市#名所・旧跡|岡谷市の名所・旧跡]] *[[岡谷市の歴史]] ==脚注== {{Reflist|2|refs= 古代諏訪物語|publisher=国書刊行会|ncid=BB07630391|ref={{Harvid|神々の里|1976年}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|year=1996|title=諏訪史蹟要項 14 岡谷市川岸篇|editor=諏訪史談会|publisher=郷土出版社|ncid=BN15058010}} * {{Cite |url=http://id.nii.ac.jp/1073/00003999/ |title=中世諏訪社の一考察 : 失われた芸能と伝承を求めて(研究プロジェクト 「諏訪学」提唱のための多角的研究) |journal=東西南北 |publisher=和光大学総合文化研究所 |volume=2016 |pages=243-206 |id={{CRID|1050001338313856640}} |ref=harv}} * {{Cite web |url=http://www.nagano-jinjacho.jp/shibu/04nansin/02suwa/4030.html |title=洩矢神社 |website=長野県神社庁 |accessdate=2018-10-01}} ==外部リンク== *[https://moriyajinja.amebaownd.com/ 洩矢神社公式HP] {{神道 横}} {{諏訪信仰}} {{デフォルトソート:もりやしんしや}} [[Category:長野県の神社]] [[Category:岡谷市の建築物]] [[Category:岡谷市の歴史]] [[Category:長野県の旧村社]] [[Category:諏訪信仰]] ==画像==

{{神社 |名称 = 洩矢神社 |画像 = [[File:洩矢神社（岡谷市）- Moriya Shrine (Okaya).jpg|280px]]
洩矢神社鳥居 {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=280|coord={{coord|36|03|04|N|138|02|10|E}}}} |所在地 = [[長野県]][[岡谷市]]川岸東1-12 |位置 = {{coord|36|03|04|N|138|02|10|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = [[洩矢神]] |社格 = [[村社]] |創建 = 不詳 |別名 = 守矢大明神、守屋大明神、洩矢大神、洩矢社 |例祭 = 10月20日（現在は10月第3日曜日）}} {{読み仮名|'''洩矢神社'''|もりやじんじゃ}}は、[[長野県]][[岡谷市]]川岸東橋原区（旧[[川岸村|橋原村]]）にある[[神社]]。[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は[[村社]]。 ==概要== [[諏訪地方]]の伝承に登場する[[洩矢神]]を祭神としている。創建年代は不詳。現在[[天竜川]]から約200メートル離れた場所にあるが、本来は川のほとりに立っていたといわれている。 現在地の周辺には[[縄文時代]]にさかのぼる複数の遺跡が確認されている。当社の西南側にある'''経塚（経塚原）遺跡'''{{Cite book|和書|year=1980|title=長野県中央道埋蔵文化財包蔵地発掘調査報告書 岡谷市その4　昭和52・53年度|editor=長野県中央道遺跡調査団|publisher=[[日本道路公団]]名古屋建設局、[[長野県教育委員会]]|chapter=第2節 経塚（経塚原）遺跡|pages=55-96|url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/en/8040}}は一時洩矢神の住居跡と信じられていたようである{{Cite book|和書|year=1924|title=諏訪史 第1巻|author=鳥居竜蔵|authorlink=鳥居竜蔵|publisher=信濃教育会諏訪部会|pages=373-374コマ目|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/935820/373}}。 ==祭神== *'''[[洩矢神]]''' :旧橋原村の[[産土神]]。諏訪大社上社に務めてきた[[守矢氏]]の遠祖とも。 ==由緒== 地域に伝わる'''[[タケミナカタ#明神入諏|諏訪明神入諏伝承]]'''によると、先住の[[国津神]]の[[洩矢神]]が諏訪に進入しようとしていた[[タケミナカタ|建御名方神]]（諏訪明神）と対抗して争ったが、戦いに敗れて降服した。建御名方神に仕える者となり、[[諏訪大社]]上社の社家[[守矢氏]]の始祖となった宮坂光昭『諏訪大社の御柱と年中行事』郷土出版社、1992年、91-93頁。{{Cite web |url=http://www.nagano-jinjacho.jp/shibu/04nansin/02suwa/4030.html |title=洩矢神社 |website=長野県神社庁 |accessdate=2018-10-01}}。 中世に書かれた文献においては諏訪明神と洩矢神が争った場所を[[守屋山]]の麓（上社本宮周辺）とされているが、[[天竜川]]のほとりを両者の戦場とする異伝は[[江戸時代]]の伝承記録に見られる{{sfn|山本ひろ子|2016|p=215-213}}。現在は洩矢神社と三沢区にある[[藤島神社 (諏訪地方)|'''藤島神社''']]が二柱の神の陣地の跡と一般的に認識されている{{Cite book|和書|author=今井野菊|year=1960|title=諏訪ものがたり|publisher=甲陽書房|pages=3-15|chapter=洩矢神と建御名方命}}{{Cite book|和書|author=今井野菊|year=1976|title=神々の里 古代諏訪物語|publisher=国書刊行会|pages=41-43|chapter=洩矢神と命の力くらべ}}{{Cite book|和書|author=守矢早苗|year=2017|title=神長官守矢史料館のしおり|edition=第三版|pages=2-3|editor=茅野市神長官守矢史料館|editor-link=神長官守矢史料館|chapter=守矢神長家のお話し}}。 なお、当社は元々[[湊村 (長野県)|花岡村]]（現・岡谷市湊）に鎮座し、この村から橋原村に移住した花岡氏を伴って遷座されたという言い伝えもある{{Cite book|和書|editor=花岡区誌編集委員会|year=1983|title=花岡区誌|chapter=第七節 民間信仰と伝承|pages=620,637}}。 ===天竜川の藤=== [[ファイル:Moriya Shrine - 洩矢神社 社殿.jpg|サムネイル|本殿を覆う[[覆堂|覆屋]]]] 洩矢神社公式HP] {{神道 横}} {{諏訪信仰}} {{デフォルトソート:もりやしんしや}} [[Category:長野県の神社]] [[Category:岡谷市の建築物]] [[Category:岡谷市の歴史]] [[Category:長野県の旧村社]] [[Category:諏訪信仰]] ==画像== [[ファイル:IMG20230327104014.jpg]]

{{神社 |名称 = 洩矢神社 |画像 = [[File:洩矢神社（岡谷市）- Moriya Shrine (Okaya).jpg|280px]]
洩矢神社鳥居 {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=280|coord={{coord|36|03|04|N|138|02|10|E}}}} |所在地 = [[長野県]][[岡谷市]]川岸東1-12 |位置 = {{coord|36|03|04|N|138|02|10|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = [[洩矢神]] |社格 = [[村社]] |創建 = 不詳 |別名 = 守矢大明神、守屋大明神、洩矢大神、洩矢社 |例祭 = 10月20日（現在は10月第3日曜日）}} {{読み仮名|'''洩矢神社'''|もりやじんじゃ}}は、[[長野県]][[岡谷市]]川岸東橋原区（旧[[川岸村|橋原村]]）にある[[神社]]。[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は[[村社]]。 ==概要== [[諏訪地方]]の伝承に登場する[[洩矢神]]を祭神としている。創建年代は不詳。現在[[天竜川]]から約200メートル離れた場所にあるが、本来は川のほとりに立っていたといわれている。 現在地の周辺には[[縄文時代]]にさかのぼる複数の遺跡が確認されている。当社の西南側にある'''経塚（経塚原）遺跡'''{{Cite book|和書|year=1980|title=長野県中央道埋蔵文化財包蔵地発掘調査報告書 岡谷市その4　昭和52・53年度|editor=長野県中央道遺跡調査団|publisher=[[日本道路公団]]名古屋建設局、[[長野県教育委員会]]|chapter=第2節 経塚（経塚原）遺跡|pages=55-96|url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/en/8040}}は一時洩矢神の住居跡と信じられていたようである{{Cite book|和書|year=1924|title=諏訪史 第1巻|author=鳥居竜蔵|authorlink=鳥居竜蔵|publisher=信濃教育会諏訪部会|pages=373-374コマ目|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/935820/373}}。 ==祭神== *'''[[洩矢神]]''' :旧橋原村の[[産土神]]。諏訪大社上社に務めてきた[[守矢氏]]の遠祖とも。 ==由緒== 地域に伝わる'''[[タケミナカタ#明神入諏|諏訪明神入諏伝承]]'''によると、先住の[[国津神]]の[[洩矢神]]が諏訪に進入しようとしていた[[タケミナカタ|建御名方神]]（諏訪明神）と対抗して争ったが、戦いに敗れて降服した。建御名方神に仕える者となり、[[諏訪大社]]上社の社家[[守矢氏]]の始祖となった宮坂光昭『諏訪大社の御柱と年中行事』郷土出版社、1992年、91-93頁。{{Cite web |url=http://www.nagano-jinjacho.jp/shibu/04nansin/02suwa/4030.html |title=洩矢神社 |website=長野県神社庁 |accessdate=2018-10-01}}。 中世に書かれた文献においては諏訪明神と洩矢神が争った場所を[[守屋山]]の麓（上社本宮周辺）とされているが、[[天竜川]]のほとりを両者の戦場とする異伝は[[江戸時代]]の伝承記録に見られる{{sfn|山本ひろ子|2016|p=215-213}}。現在は洩矢神社と三沢区にある[[藤島神社 (諏訪地方)|'''藤島神社''']]が二柱の神の陣地の跡と一般的に認識されている{{Cite book|和書|author=今井野菊|year=1960|title=諏訪ものがたり|publisher=甲陽書房|pages=3-15|chapter=洩矢神と建御名方命}}{{Cite book|和書|author=今井野菊|year=1976|title=神々の里 古代諏訪物語|publisher=国書刊行会|pages=41-43|chapter=洩矢神と命の力くらべ}}{{Cite book|和書|author=守矢早苗|year=2017|title=神長官守矢史料館のしおり|edition=第三版|pages=2-3|editor=茅野市神長官守矢史料館|editor-link=神長官守矢史料館|chapter=守矢神長家のお話し}}。 なお、当社は元々[[湊村 (長野県)|花岡村]]（現・岡谷市湊）に鎮座し、この村から橋原村に移住した花岡氏を伴って遷座されたという言い伝えもある{{Cite book|和書|editor=花岡区誌編集委員会|year=1983|title=花岡区誌|chapter=第七節 民間信仰と伝承|pages=620,637}}。 ===天竜川の藤=== [[ファイル:Moriya Shrine - 洩矢神社 社殿.jpg|サムネイル|本殿を覆う[[覆堂|覆屋]]]] 言い伝えによると、洩矢社は元々は天竜川のほとりにあった。境内にある藤の木が対岸の藤島神社の藤の木と絡み合い、大きい橋に見えるほど繁茂していた。[[寛文]]年間（1661年～1673年）の頃、[[諏訪藩|諏訪藩主]]は天竜川の蛍狩り遊覧のため藤を伐り払うように命じたが、人々は神の祟りを恐れて伐る者がいなかった。新屋敷の小石嘉右衛門という傲慢な人が山役の料（[[蔵米]]三[[升]]）二人分を条件に藤を伐採したところ、間もなく気がおかしくなってしまった。京都の[[吉田家]]に祈祷してもらい少し収まったが、ある日、祈祷の札を頭にのせて家を飛び出し、「半の木」という山腰（現・岡谷市半ノ木か）で突然倒れて死んだ。神罰を恐れた人々はその場所に神札を祀って「鎮目大明神」と名づけた。 命令を下した藩主にも祟りがあったため、お詫びに[[高島城|城内]]で祠を作らせて奉納することにしたが、あまりにも大きすぎて大手門を出ることができなかった。やむなく少し縮めて奉納されたのが現在の本殿であるといわれている。この際に当社を現在地へ遷座し、藩主が神事免を寄付した。それ以降は当社が諏訪藩主に篤く崇敬され、[[明治維新]]期の[[廃藩置県]]まで例祭は藩主によって行われた{{sfn|山本ひろ子|2016|p=213-212}}。 ==神徳== 霊験あらたかな神とされ、特に産婦が[[柄杓#柄杓に関する事柄|底抜けの柄杓]]を献納すれば安産ができるといわれている。 ==祭事== *[[元始祭]] *[[虫送り|除蝗祭]] *'''例祭'''（[[10月20日]]洩矢神社由緒書き看板。、現在は10月第3日曜日） *[[二年参り]]（12月31日） *'''[[御柱祭#小宮祭|御柱祭]]'''（寅年・申年） *:古来は諏訪大社下社の御柱祭と同日に行われたが、近年では諸事情により他の日で行なわれている。 なお、諏訪大社との深い関わりから、大社の[[幟|織旗]]と同じ太さのものを許されている。 ==交通アクセス== *[[東日本旅客鉄道|JR東日本]][[中央本線]] [[岡谷駅]]より徒歩約15分（約1.1km） * [[シルキーバス]]川岸橋原線 「洩矢社前」バス停より徒歩1分 ==関連場所== ===洩矢大神御旧趾碑=== [[ファイル:Moriya-no-Ōkami Gokyūseki - 洩矢大神御舊趾.jpg|サムネイル|200px|洩矢大神御舊趾]] 当社から約300m（徒歩5分）離れた位置にある石碑。碑銘は神長官・守矢実顕（第75代、1825年～1903年）の揮毫によるもの。遷座記念碑によると元々は「洩矢大神の住居跡」（上述の経塚遺跡）にあったが、昭和53年（1978年）に[[中央自動車道]]の建設事業により現在地に移された。 '''位置：''' {{coord|36|02|56|N|138|02|05|E|region:JP}} ===龍源山の神=== 洩矢神社から約1.3km（徒歩19分）離れた岡谷市湊（旧花岡村）にある[[祠]]であり、昔は「'''左山神'''（ひだりやまのかみ）」と呼ばれていた。祭神は[[オオヤマツミ|大山祇神]]。 当地の伝承によれば、かつては洩矢神がこの周辺にあった部落の人々に[[産土神]]として崇められていた。二十数軒もある部落であったが、立地条件に恵まれていないため、[[明暦]]（1655年～1658年）の頃から人々が段々とここを離れ、他所へ移動していた。花岡姓を名乗る一軒が隣の橋原村へ引っ越した際に、ここに祀られていた洩矢神を橋原のほうに遷座し、その社の跡に[[山の神]]を祀って松の木を植えた。木の枝が栄えたため、「{{読み仮名|枝垂|しだ}}れ山の神」（後に「左山の神」に転化）と呼ばれるようになったという。 中央道の通過により、その工事用道路として拡幅があり、旧地から一段上へ移転された。 '''位置：'''{{coord|36|03|09|N|138|02|53|E|region:JP}} [[File:Ryūgen Yama-no-Kami - 龍源山の神.jpg|サムネイル|左|140px|龍源山の神]] ==関連項目== *[[諏訪大社]] *[[守矢氏]] *[[守屋山]] *[[藤島神社 (諏訪地方)]] *[[小野神社・矢彦神社]] - 洩矢神と戦う前に建御名方神がしばらく滞在したという伝承を持つ神社 *[[東方風神録 〜 Mountain of Faith.]] - ゲーム内に洩矢神に由来する登場人物があるため、そのファンの「[[巡礼 (通俗)|聖地]]」となっている{{cite web|url=https://www.asahi.com/articles/ASKB66VTHKB6UOOB00T.html|title=ゲーム人気で聖地に　岡谷の洩矢神社|publisher=[[朝日新聞]]|date=2017-10-28|accessdate=2018-10-03}}{{cite web|url=http://www.shimin.co.jp/2017/12/%E3%82%B2%E3%83%BC%E3%83%A0%E5%88%87%E3%81%A3%E6%8E%9B%E3%81%91%E9%96%A2%E5%BF%83%E6%9B%B4%E3%81%AB%e3%80%80%E6%A9%8B%E5%8E%9F%E6%B4%A9%E7%9F%A2%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E3%81%AB%E5%A4%9A%E3%81%8F%E3%81%AE/|title=ゲーム切っ掛け関心更に　橋原洩矢神社に多くのファン|website=信州・市民新聞グループ|date=2017-12-01|publisher=株式会社岡谷市民新聞社|accessdate=2018-10-03}}{{cite web|url=https://moriyajinja.amebaownd.com/posts/1644415|title=東方projectの聖地巡礼らしいです|website=洩矢神社公式HP|date=2016-11-22|accessdate=2018-10-03}} *[[岡谷市#名所・旧跡|岡谷市の名所・旧跡]] *[[岡谷市の歴史]] ==脚注== {{Reflist|2|refs= {{Cite book|和書|year=1996|title=諏訪史蹟要項 14 岡谷市川岸篇|editor=諏訪史談会|publisher=郷土出版社}} }} == 参考文献 == * {{Cite 古代諏訪物語|publisher=国書刊行会|ncid=BB07630391|ref={{Harvid|神々の里|1976年}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|year=1996|title=諏訪史蹟要項 14 岡谷市川岸篇|editor=諏訪史談会|publisher=郷土出版社|ncid=BN15058010}} * {{Cite 岡谷市その4　昭和52・53年度|editor=長野県中央道遺跡調査団|publisher=[[日本道路公団]]名古屋建設局、[[長野県教育委員会]]|url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/en/8040|ncid=BN10347236}} * {{Cite 現在地の周辺には[[縄文時代]]にさかのぼる複数の遺跡が確認されている。当社の西南側にある'''経塚（経塚原）遺跡'''{{Cite book|和書|year=1980|title=長野県中央道埋蔵文化財包蔵地発掘調査報告書 岡谷市その4　昭和52・53年度|editor=長野県中央道遺跡調査団|publisher=[[日本道路公団]]名古屋建設局、[[長野県教育委員会]]|chapter=第2節 経塚（経塚原）遺跡|pages=55-96|url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/en/8040}}は一時洩矢神の住居跡と信じられていたようである{{Cite book|和書|year=1924|title=諏訪史 第1巻|author=鳥居竜蔵|authorlink=鳥居竜蔵|publisher=信濃教育会諏訪部会|pages=373-374コマ目|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/935820/373}}。 ==祭神== *'''[[洩矢神]]''' :旧橋原村の[[産土神]]。諏訪大社上社に務めてきた[[守矢氏]]の遠祖とも。 ==由緒== 地域に伝わる'''[[タケミナカタ#明神入諏|諏訪明神入諏伝承]]'''によると、先住の[[国津神]]の[[洩矢神]]が諏訪に進入しようとしていた[[タケミナカタ|建御名方神]]（諏訪明神）と対抗して争ったが、戦いに敗れて降服した。建御名方神に仕える者となり、[[諏訪大社]]上社の社家[[守矢氏]]の始祖となった宮坂光昭『諏訪大社の御柱と年中行事』郷土出版社、1992年、91-93頁。{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.nagano-jinjacho.jp/shibu/04nansin/02suwa/4030.html |title=洩矢神社 |website=長野県神社庁 |accessdate=2018-10-01}}。 中世に書かれた文献においては諏訪明神と洩矢神が争った場所を[[守屋山]]の麓（上社本宮周辺）とされているが、[[天竜川]]のほとりを両者の戦場とする異伝は[[江戸時代]]の伝承記録に見られる{{sfn|山本ひろ子|2016|p=215-213}}。現在は洩矢神社と三沢区にある[[藤島神社 (諏訪地方)|'''藤島神社''']]が二柱の神の陣地の跡と一般的に認識されている{{Cite book|和書|author=今井野菊|year=1960|title=諏訪ものがたり|publisher=甲陽書房|pages=3-15|chapter=洩矢神と建御名方命}}{{Cite book|和書|author=今井野菊|year=1976|title=神々の里 古代諏訪物語|publisher=国書刊行会|pages=41-43|chapter=洩矢神と命の力くらべ}}{{Cite book|和書|author=守矢早苗|year=2017|title=神長官守矢史料館のしおり|edition=第三版|pages=2-3|editor=茅野市神長官守矢史料館|editor-link=神長官守矢史料館|chapter=守矢神長家のお話し}}。 なお、当社は元々[[湊村 (長野県)|花岡村]]（現・岡谷市湊）に鎮座し、この村から橋原村に移住した花岡氏を伴って遷座されたという言い伝えもある{{Cite book|和書|editor=花岡区誌編集委員会|year=1983|title=花岡区誌|chapter=第七節 民間信仰と伝承|pages=620,637}}。 ===天竜川の藤=== [[ファイル:Moriya Shrine - 洩矢神社 社殿.jpg|サムネイル|本殿を覆う[[覆堂|覆屋]]]] 言い伝えによると、洩矢社は元々は天竜川のほとりにあった。境内にある藤の木が対岸の藤島神社の藤の木と絡み合い、大きい橋に見えるほど繁茂していた。[[寛文]]年間（1661年～1673年）の頃、[[諏訪藩|諏訪藩主]]は天竜川の蛍狩り遊覧のため藤を伐り払うように命じたが、人々は神の祟りを恐れて伐る者がいなかった。新屋敷の小石嘉右衛門という傲慢な人が山役の料（[[蔵米]]三[[升]]）二人分を条件に藤を伐採したところ、間もなく気がおかしくなってしまった。京都の[[吉田家]]に祈祷してもらい少し収まったが、ある日、祈祷の札を頭にのせて家を飛び出し、「半の木」という山腰（現・岡谷市半ノ木か）で突然倒れて死んだ。神罰を恐れた人々はその場所に神札を祀って「鎮目大明神」と名づけた。 命令を下した藩主にも祟りがあったため、お詫びに[[高島城|城内]]で祠を作らせて奉納することにしたが、あまりにも大きすぎて大手門を出ることができなかった。やむなく少し縮めて奉納されたのが現在の本殿であるといわれている。この際に当社を現在地へ遷座し、藩主が神事免を寄付した。それ以降は当社が諏訪藩主に篤く崇敬され、[[明治維新]]期の[[廃藩置県]]まで例祭は藩主によって行われた{{sfn|山本ひろ子|2016|p=213-212}}。 ==神徳== 霊験あらたかな神とされ、特に産婦が[[柄杓#柄杓に関する事柄|底抜けの柄杓]]を献納すれば安産ができるといわれている。 ==祭事== *[[元始祭]] *[[虫送り|除蝗祭]] *'''例祭'''（[[10月20日]]洩矢神社由緒書き看板。、現在は10月第3日曜日） *[[二年参り]]（12月31日） *'''[[御柱祭#小宮祭|御柱祭]]'''（寅年・申年） *:古来は諏訪大社下社の御柱祭と同日に行われたが、近年では諸事情により他の日で行なわれている。 なお、諏訪大社との深い関わりから、大社の[[幟|織旗]]と同じ太さのものを許されている。 ==交通アクセス== *[[東日本旅客鉄道|JR東日本]][[中央本線]] [[岡谷駅]]より徒歩約15分（約1.1km） * [[シルキーバス]]川岸橋原線 「洩矢社前」バス停より徒歩1分 ==関連場所== ===洩矢大神御旧趾碑=== [[ファイル:Moriya-no-Ōkami Gokyūseki - 洩矢大神御舊趾.jpg|サムネイル|200px|洩矢大神御舊趾]] 当社から約300m（徒歩5分）離れた位置にある石碑。碑銘は神長官・守矢実顕（第75代、1825年～1903年）の揮毫によるもの。遷座記念碑によると元々は「洩矢大神の住居跡」（上述の経塚遺跡）にあったが、昭和53年（1978年）に[[中央自動車道]]の建設事業により現在地に移された。 '''位置：''' {{coord|36|02|56|N|138|02|05|E|region:JP}} ===龍源山の神=== 洩矢神社から約1.3km（徒歩19分）離れた岡谷市湊（旧花岡村）にある[[祠]]であり、昔は「'''左山神'''（ひだりやまのかみ）」と呼ばれていた。祭神は[[オオヤマツミ|大山祇神]]。 当地の伝承によれば、かつては洩矢神がこの周辺にあった部落の人々に[[産土神]]として崇められていた。二十数軒もある部落であったが、立地条件に恵まれていないため、[[明暦]]（1655年～1658年）の頃から人々が段々とここを離れ、他所へ移動していた。花岡姓を名乗る一軒が隣の橋原村へ引っ越した際に、ここに祀られていた洩矢神を橋原のほうに遷座し、その社の跡に[[山の神]]を祀って松の木を植えた。木の枝が栄えたため、「{{読み仮名|枝垂|しだ}}れ山の神」（後に「左山の神」に転化）と呼ばれるようになったという。 中央道の通過により、その工事用道路として拡幅があり、旧地から一段上へ移転された。 '''位置：'''{{coord|36|03|09|N|138|02|53|E|region:JP}} [[File:Ryūgen Yama-no-Kami - 龍源山の神.jpg|サムネイル|左|140px|龍源山の神]] ==関連項目== *[[諏訪大社]] *[[守矢氏]] *[[守屋山]] *[[藤島神社 (諏訪地方)]] *[[小野神社・矢彦神社]] - 洩矢神と戦う前に建御名方神がしばらく滞在したという伝承を持つ神社 *[[東方風神録 〜 Mountain of Faith.]] - ゲーム内に洩矢神に由来する登場人物があるため、そのファンの「[[巡礼 (通俗)|聖地]]」となっている{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.asahi.com/articles/ASKB66VTHKB6UOOB00T.html|title=ゲーム人気で聖地に　岡谷の洩矢神社|publisher=[[朝日新聞]]|date=2017-10-28|accessdate=2018-10-03}}{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.shimin.co.jp/2017/12/%E3%82%B2%E3%83%BC%E3%83%A0%E5%88%87%E3%81%A3%E6%8E%9B%E3%81%91%E9%96%A2%E5%BF%83%E6%9B%B4%E3%81%AB%e3%80%80%E6%A9%8B%E5%8E%9F%E6%B4%A9%E7%9F%A2%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E3%81%AB%E5%A4%9A%E3%81%8F%E3%81%AE/|title=ゲーム切っ掛け関心更に　橋原洩矢神社に多くのファン|website=信州・市民新聞グループ|date=2017-12-01|publisher=株式会社岡谷市民新聞社|accessdate=2018-10-03}}{{Cite web|和書|url=https://moriyajinja.amebaownd.com/posts/1644415|title=東方projectの聖地巡礼らしいです|website=洩矢神社公式HP|date=2016-11-22|accessdate=2018-10-03}} *[[岡谷市#名所・旧跡|岡谷市の名所・旧跡]] *[[岡谷市の歴史]] ==脚注== {{Reflist|2|refs= {{Cite book|和書|year=1996|title=諏訪史蹟要項 14 岡谷市川岸篇|editor=諏訪史談会|publisher=郷土出版社}} }} == 参考文献 == * {{Cite book|和書|author=今井野菊|year=1960|title=諏訪ものがたり|publisher=甲陽書房|ncid=BA91147253|ref={{Harvid|諏訪ものがたり|1960年}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|author=今井野菊|year=1976|title=神々の里 古代諏訪物語|publisher=国書刊行会|ncid=BB07630391|ref={{Harvid|神々の里|1976年}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|year=1996|title=諏訪史蹟要項 14 岡谷市川岸篇|editor=諏訪史談会|publisher=郷土出版社|ncid=BN15058010}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=茅野市神長官守矢史料館|year=2017|title=神長官守矢史料館のしおり|edition=第三版}} * {{Cite book|和書|year=1980|title=長野県中央道埋蔵文化財包蔵地発掘調査報告書 岡谷市その4　昭和52・53年度|editor=長野県中央道遺跡調査団|publisher=[[日本道路公団]]名古屋建設局、[[長野県教育委員会]]|url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/en/8040|ncid=BN10347236}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=花岡区誌編集委員会|year=1983|title=花岡区誌|ncid=BN13324962|ref={{Harvid|花岡区誌|1983年}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|author=宮坂光昭|year=1992|title=諏訪大社の御柱と年中行事|publisher=郷土出版社|isbn=978-4-876-63178-0|ref={{Harvid|諏訪大社の御柱と年中行事|1992年}}}} * {{Cite journal|和書|author=山本ひろ子 |date=2016-03 |url=http://id.nii.ac.jp/1073/00003999/ |title=中世諏訪社の一考察 : 失われた芸能と伝承を求めて(研究プロジェクト 「諏訪学」提唱のための多角的研究) |journal=東西南北 |publisher=和光大学総合文化研究所 |volume=2016 |pages=243-206 |id={{CRID|1050001338313856640}} |ref=harv}} * {{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.nagano-jinjacho.jp/shibu/04nansin/02suwa/4030.html |title=洩矢神社 |website=長野県神社庁 |accessdate=2018-10-01}} ==外部リンク== *[https://moriyajinja.amebaownd.com/ {{Shrine |名称 = Moriya Shrine |画像 = [[File:洩矢神社（岡谷市）- Moriya Shrine (Okaya).jpg|280px]]
Moriya Shrine Torii {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=280|coord={{coord|36|03|04|N|138|02|10|E}}}} |所在地 = {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}, {{ill|Okaya, Nagano|ja|岡谷市}}, Kawagishi Higashi 1-12 |位置 = {{coord|36|03|04|N|138|02|10|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|Moriya no Kami|ja|洩矢神}} |社格 = {{ill|Village shrine (Japan)|ja|村社}} |創建 = Unknown |別名 = Moriya Daimyōjin, Moriyama Daimyōjin, Moriya Ōkami, Moriya Shrine |例祭 = October 20 (Currently the third Sunday in October) }} {{読み仮名|'''Moriya Shrine'''|もりやじんじゃ}} is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Okaya, Nagano|ja|岡谷市}}, Nagano Prefecture, in the Kawagishi Higashi Hashiwara district (formerly {{ill|Kawagishi Village|ja|川岸村|橋原村}}). The shrine's former classification under the {{ill|modern shrine ranking system|ja|近代社格制度}} was that of a {{ill|Village shrine (Japan)|ja|村社}}. == Overview == The shrine enshrines {{ill|Moriya no Kami|ja|洩矢神}}, a deity from {{ill|Suwa|ja|諏訪地方}} traditions. Its exact date of establishment is unknown. The shrine currently stands approximately 200 meters from the {{ill|Tenryū River|ja|天竜川}}, though it was originally said to be located by the riverbank. Archaeological evidence suggests that the area surrounding the shrine has been inhabited since the {{ill|Jōmon period|ja|縄文時代}}. Near the shrine's southwestern side lies the "Kyōzuka (Kyōzukahara) Site" {{Cite book|和書|year=1980|title=長野県中央道埋蔵文化財包蔵地発掘調査報告書 岡谷市その4 昭和52・53年度|editor=長野県中央道遺跡調査団|publisher=[[Japan Highway Public Corporation]] Nagoya Construction Bureau, [[Nagano Prefectural Board of Education]]|chapter=第2節 経塚（経塚原）遺跡|pages=55-96|url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/en/8040}}, once believed to be the dwelling of {{ill|Moriya no Kami|ja|洩矢神}}.{{Cite book|和書|year=1924|title=諏訪史 第1巻|author=Torii Ryūzō|authorlink=Torii Ryūzō|publisher=Shinano Educational Society Suwa Branch|pages=373-374|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/935820/373}} == Deity == * '''{{ill|Moriya no Kami|ja|洩矢神}}''' : The local {{ill|Ujigami|ja|産土神}} of the former Hashiwara Village and a distant ancestor of the {{ill|Moriya clan|ja|守矢氏}}, historically associated with the {{ill|Suwa Taisha Upper Shrine|ja|諏訪大社#上社}}. == History == According to the {{ill|Takeminakata#Mythological entry into Suwa|ja|タケミナカタ#明神入諏|Suwa Myths}}, Moriya no Kami, an indigenous {{ill|Kunitsukami|ja|国津神}}, opposed the advance of {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方神}} (the Suwa deity) into Suwa. Following a battle, Moriya no Kami was defeated and submitted to Takeminakata, becoming his vassal and the progenitor of the {{ill|Moriya clan|ja|守矢氏}}, who served the Suwa Upper Shrine.Miyasaka Mitsuaki, ''Suwa Taisha no Onbashira to Nenchū Gyōji'' (郷土出版社, 1992), pp. 91-93.{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.nagano-jinjacho.jp/shibu/04nansin/02suwa/4030.html|title=Moriya Shrine|website=Nagano Shrine Association|accessdate=2018-10-01}} Medieval texts locate the confrontation between the two deities near {{ill|Mount Moriya|ja|守屋山}}, around the {{ill|Upper Shrine Main Sanctuary|ja|諏訪大社#上社|本宮}} area. However, later Edo-period traditions identify the {{ill|Tenryū River|ja|天竜川}} banks as their battleground. Today, Moriya Shrine and nearby {{ill|Fujishima Shrine (Suwa)|ja|藤島神社 (諏訪地方)}} are recognized as the sites of their respective camps.{{Cite book|和書|author=Imai Nokiku|year=1960|title=Suwa Monogatari|publisher=Kōyō Shobō|pages=3-15|chapter=Moriya no Kami and Takeminakata}}{{Cite book|和書|author=Imai Nokiku|year=1976|title=The Land of the Gods: Ancient Suwa Tales|publisher=Kokusho Kankōkai|pages=41-43}}{{Cite book|和書|author=Moriya Sanae|year=2017|title=The Guide to the Chino Moriya Archive|edition=Third Edition|pages=2-3|editor=Chino Moriya Archive|editor-link=神長官守矢史料館}} It is said that the shrine originally stood in Hanoka Village (now Okaya's Minato district). When residents moved to Hashiwara Village, they brought Moriya Shrine with them.{{Cite book|和書|editor=Hanoka District History Committee|year=1983|title=Hanoka District History|chapter=民間信仰と伝承|pages=620,637}} === The Fuji of the Tenryū River === A legend recounts that Moriya Shrine was once located by the riverbank. Wisteria trees in its precincts grew intertwined with those across the river at Fujishima Shrine, creating a bridge-like canopy. During the Kanbun era (1661–1673), the {{ill|Suwa Domain|ja|諏訪藩}} lord ordered their removal for a firefly-viewing event. However, local villagers feared divine retribution and refused. A man named Koishi Kaemon took on the task for a fee but soon fell into madness and met a tragic end, further cementing the wisteria’s sacred status. == Worship and Rituals == * [[Genshi-sai]] (元始祭) * [[Insect-Sending Festival]] (虫送り祭) * **Annual Festival** (例祭): October 20 or the third Sunday in October * [[Ninenmairi]] (二年参り): December 31 * **Onbashira Festival** (御柱祭): Conducted in years of the Tiger and Monkey : Historically aligned with the {{ill|Lower Shrine|ja|諏訪大社#下社}} festival but now held on separate dates. == Access == * 15 minutes (1.1 km) on foot from {{ill|Okaya Station|ja|岡谷駅}}, JR {{ill|Chūō Main Line|ja|中央本線}} * 1 minute from "Moriya Shrine Front" bus stop, {{ill|Silky Bus Kawagishi Line|ja|シルキーバス川岸橋原線}} == Related Sites == === Former Moriya Ōkami Site === Located 300m from the shrine, marked by a stone monument with calligraphy by Moriya Jikken, the 75th shrine chief. === Ryūgen Mountain Deity === A small shrine about 1.3 km from Moriya Shrine, dedicated to {{ill|Ōyama-tsumi|ja|大山祇神}}. The area was once part of Moriya no Kami’s sphere of influence. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://moriyajinja.amebaownd.com/ Official Moriya Shrine Website] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Suwa Faith}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Moriya Shrine}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Okaya, Nagano]] [[Category:Suwa Faith]] [[Category:Former village shrines]] [[Category:History of Okaya, Nagano]] [[Category:Tenryū River]] ==References== {{reflist}} {{Suwa Faith}} [[ja:洩矢神社]][[Category:Suwa Faith topics in the template that need translation to English]] [[Category:Suwa shrines]] [[Category:original pages]] [[en:Morikawa Shrine]]

{{Shrine |名称 = Moriya Shrine |画像 = [[File:洩矢神社（岡谷市）- Moriya Shrine (Okaya).jpg|280px]]
Moriya Shrine Torii {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=280|coord={{coord|36|03|04|N|138|02|10|E}}}} |所在地 = {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}, {{ill|Okaya, Nagano|ja|岡谷市}}, Kawagishi Higashi 1-12 |位置 = {{coord|36|03|04|N|138|02|10|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|Moriya no Kami|ja|洩矢神}} |社格 = {{ill|Village shrine (Japan)|ja|村社}} |創建 = Unknown |別名 = Moriya Daimyōjin, Moriyama Daimyōjin, Moriya Ōkami, Moriya Shrine |例祭 = October 20 (Currently the third Sunday in October) }} '''Moriya Shrine''' (読み仮名) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Okaya, Nagano|ja|岡谷市}}, Nagano Prefecture, in the Kawagishi Higashi Hashiwara district (formerly {{ill|Kawagishi Village|ja|川岸村|橋原村}}). The shrine's former classification under the {{ill|modern shrine ranking system|ja|近代社格制度}} was that of a {{ill|Village shrine (Japan)|ja|村社}}. == Overview == The shrine enshrines {{ill|Moriya no Kami|ja|洩矢神}}, a deity from {{ill|Suwa|ja|諏訪地方}} traditions. Its exact date of establishment is unknown. The shrine currently stands approximately 200 meters from the {{ill|Tenryū River|ja|天竜川}}, though it was originally said to be located by the riverbank. Archaeological evidence suggests that the area surrounding the shrine has been inhabited since the {{ill|Jōmon period|ja|縄文時代}}. Following a battle, Moriya no Kami was defeated and submitted to Takeminakata, becoming his vassal and the progenitor of the {{ill|Moriya clan|ja|守矢氏}}, who served the Suwa Upper Shrine.Miyasaka Mitsuaki, ''Suwa Taisha no Onbashira to Nenchū Gyōji'' (郷土出版社, 1992), pp. 91-93.{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.nagano-jinjacho.jp/shibu/04nansin/02suwa/4030.html|title=Moriya Shrine|website=Nagano Shrine Association|accessdate=2018-10-01}} Medieval texts locate the confrontation between the two deities near {{ill|Mount Moriya|ja|守屋山}}, around the {{ill|Upper Shrine Main Sanctuary|ja|諏訪大社#上社|本宮}} area. However, later Edo-period traditions identify the {{ill|Tenryū River|ja|天竜川}} banks as their battleground. Today, Moriya Shrine and nearby {{ill|Fujishima Shrine (Suwa)|ja|藤島神社 (諏訪地方)}} are recognized as the sites of their respective camps.{{Cite book|和書|author=Imai Nokiku|year=1960|title=Suwa Monogatari|publisher=Kōyō Shobō|pages=3-15|chapter=Moriya no Kami and Takeminakata}}{{Cite book|和書|author=Imai Nokiku|year=1976|title=The Land of the Gods: Ancient Suwa Tales|publisher=Kokusho Kankōkai|pages=41-43}}{{Cite book|和書|author=Moriya Sanae|year=2017|title=The Guide to the Chino Moriya Archive|edition=Third Edition|pages=2-3|editor=Chino Moriya Archive|editor-link=神長官守矢史料館}} It is said that the shrine originally stood in Hanoka Village (now Okaya's Minato district). When residents moved to Hashiwara Village, they brought Moriya Shrine with them.{{Cite book|和書|editor=Hanoka District History Committee|year=1983|title=Hanoka District History|chapter=民間信仰と伝承|pages=620,637}} === The Fuji of the Tenryū River === A legend recounts that Moriya Shrine was once located by the riverbank. The area was once part of Moriya no Kami’s sphere of influence. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://moriyajinja.amebaownd.com/ Official Moriya Shrine Website] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Suwa Faith}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Moriya Shrine}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Okaya, Nagano]] [[Category:Suwa Faith]] [[Category:Former village shrines]] [[Category:History of Okaya, Nagano]] [[Category:Tenryū River]] ==References== {{reflist}} {{Suwa Faith}} [[ja:洩矢神社]][[Category:Suwa Faith topics in the template that need translation to English]] [[Category:Suwa shrines]] [[Category:original pages]] [[en:Morikawa Shrine]]

{{Shrine |name = Moriya Shrine |image = [[File:洩矢神社（岡谷市）- Moriya Shrine (Okaya).jpg|280px]]

However, later Edo-period traditions identify the {{ill|Tenryū River|ja|天竜川}} banks as their battleground. Today, Moriya Shrine and nearby {{ill|Fujishima Shrine (Suwa)|ja|藤島神社 (諏訪地方)}} are recognized as the sites of their respective camps.{{Cite book|和書|author=Imai Nokiku|year=1960|title=Suwa Monogatari|publisher=Kōyō Shobō|pages=3-15|chapter=Moriya no Kami and Takeminakata}}{{Cite book|和書|author=Imai Nokiku|year=1976|title=The Land of the Gods: Ancient Suwa Tales|publisher=Kokusho Kankōkai|pages=41-43}}{{Cite book|和書|author=Moriya Sanae|year=2017|title=The Guide to the Chino Moriya Archive|edition=Third Edition|pages=2-3|editor=Chino Moriya Archive|editor-link=神長官守矢史料館}} It is said that the shrine originally stood in Hanoka Village (now Okaya's Minato district). When residents moved to Hashiwara Village, they brought Moriya Shrine with them.{{Cite book|和書|editor=Hanoka District History Committee|year=1983|title=Hanoka District History|chapter=民間信仰と伝承|pages=620,637}} === The Fuji of the Tenryū River === A legend recounts that Moriya Shrine was once located by the riverbank. Wisteria trees in its precincts grew intertwined with those across the river at Fujishima Shrine, creating a bridge-like canopy. During the Kanbun era (1661–1673), the {{ill|Suwa Domain|ja|諏訪藩}} lord ordered their removal for a firefly-viewing event. However, local villagers feared divine retribution and refused. A man named Koishi Kaemon took on the task for a fee but soon fell into madness and met a tragic end, further cementing the wisteria’s sacred status. == Worship and Rituals == * [[Genshi-sai]] (元始祭) * [[Insect-Sending Festival]] (虫送り祭) * **Annual Festival** (festival): October 20 or the third Sunday in October * [[Ninenmairi]] (二年参り): December 31 * **Onbashira Festival** (御柱祭): Conducted in years of the Tiger and Monkey : Historically aligned with the {{ill|Lower Shrine|ja|諏訪大社#下社}} festival but now held on separate dates. == Access == * 15 minutes (1.1 km) on foot from {{ill|Okaya Station|ja|岡谷駅}}, JR {{ill|Chūō Main Line|ja|中央本線}} * 1 minute from "Moriya Shrine Front" bus stop, {{ill|Silky Bus Kawagishi Line|ja|シルキーバス川岸橋原線}} == Related Sites == === Former Moriya Ōkami Site === Located 300m from the shrine, marked by a stone monument with calligraphy by Moriya Jikken, the 75th shrine chief. === Ryūgen Mountain Deity === A small shrine about 1.3 km from Moriya Shrine, dedicated to {{ill|Ōyama-tsumi|ja|大山祇神}}. The area was once part of Moriya no Kami’s sphere of influence. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://moriyajinja.amebaownd.com/ Official Moriya Shrine Website] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Suwa Faith}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Moriya Shrine}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Okaya, Nagano]] [[Category:Suwa Faith]] [[Category:Former village shrines]] [[Category:History of Okaya, Nagano]] [[Category:Tenryū River]] ==References== {{reflist}} {{Suwa Faith}} [[ja:洩矢神社]][[Category:Suwa faith]] [[Category:Suwa shrines]] [[Category:original pages]] [[en:Morikawa Shrine]]

{{Shrine |name = Moriya Shrine |image = [[File:洩矢神社（岡谷市）- Moriya Shrine (Okaya).jpg|280px]]
Moriya Shrine Torii {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=280|coord={{coord|36|03|04|N|138|02|10|E}}}} |location = {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}, {{ill|Okaya, Nagano|ja|岡谷市}}, Kawagishi Higashi 1-12 |coordinates = {{coord|36|03|04|N|138|02|10|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Moriya no Kami|ja|洩矢神}} |type = {{ill|Village shrine (Japan)|ja|村社}} |創建 = Unknown |alternative names = Moriya Daimyōjin, Moriyama Daimyōjin, Moriya Ōkami, Moriya Shrine |festival = October 20 (Currently the third Sunday in October) }} '''Moriya Shrine''' (読み仮名) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Okaya, Nagano|ja|岡谷市}}, Nagano Prefecture, in the Kawagishi Higashi Hashiwara district (formerly {{ill|Kawagishi Village|ja|川岸村|橋原村}}). The shrine's former classification under the {{ill|modern shrine ranking system|ja|近代type制度}} was that of a {{ill|Village shrine (Japan)|ja|村社}}. == Overview == The shrine enshrines {{ill|Moriya no Kami|ja|洩矢神}}, a deity from {{ill|Suwa|ja|諏訪地方}} traditions. Its exact date of establishment is unknown. The shrine currently stands approximately 200 meters from the {{ill|Tenryū River|ja|天竜川}}, though it was originally said to be located by the riverbank. Archaeological evidence suggests that the area surrounding the shrine has been inhabited since the {{ill|Jōmon period|ja|縄文時代}}. Near the shrine's southwestern side lies the "Kyōzuka (Kyōzukahara) Site" {{Cite book|和書|year=1980|title=長野県中央道埋蔵文化財包蔵地発掘調査報告書 岡谷市その4 昭和52・53年度|editor=長野県中央道遺跡調査団|publisher=[[Japan Highway Public Corporation]] Nagoya Construction Bureau, [[Nagano Prefectural Board of Education]]|chapter=第2節 経塚（経塚原）遺跡|pages=55-96|url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/en/8040}}, once believed to be the dwelling of {{ill|Moriya no Kami|ja|洩矢神}}.{{Cite book|和書|year=1924|title=諏訪史 第1巻|author=Torii Ryūzō|authorlink=Torii Ryūzō|publisher=Shinano Educational Society Suwa Branch|pages=373-374|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/935820/373}} == Deity == * '''{{ill|Moriya no Kami|ja|洩矢神}}''' : The local {{ill|Ujigami|ja|産土神}} of the former Hashiwara Village and a distant ancestor of the {{ill|Moriya clan|ja|守矢氏}}, historically associated with the {{ill|Suwa Taisha Upper Shrine|ja|諏訪大社#上社}}. == History == According to the {{ill|Takeminakata#Mythological entry into Suwa|ja|タケミナカタ#明神入諏|Suwa Myths}}, Moriya no Kami, an indigenous {{ill|Kunitsukami|ja|国津神}}, opposed the advance of {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方神}} (the Suwa deity) into Suwa. Following a battle, Moriya no Kami was defeated and submitted to Takeminakata, becoming his vassal and the progenitor of the {{ill|Moriya clan|ja|守矢氏}}, who served the Suwa Upper Shrine.Miyasaka Mitsuaki, ''Suwa Taisha no Onbashira to Nenchū Gyōji'' (郷土出版社, 1992), pp. 91-93.{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.nagano-jinjacho.jp/shibu/04nansin/02suwa/4030.html|title=Moriya Shrine|website=Nagano Shrine Association|accessdate=2018-10-01}} Medieval texts locate the confrontation between the two deities near {{ill|Mount Moriya|ja|守屋山}}, around the {{ill|Upper Shrine Main Sanctuary|ja|諏訪大社#上社|本宮}} area. However, later Edo-period traditions identify the {{ill|Tenryū River|ja|天竜川}} banks as their battleground. Today, Moriya Shrine and nearby {{ill|Fujishima Shrine (Suwa)|ja|藤島神社 (諏訪地方)}} are recognized as the sites of their respective camps.{{Cite book|和書|author=Imai Nokiku|year=1960|title=Suwa Monogatari|publisher=Kōyō Shobō|pages=3-15|chapter=Moriya no Kami and Takeminakata}}{{Cite book|和書|author=Imai Nokiku|year=1976|title=The Land of the Gods: Ancient Suwa Tales|publisher=Kokusho Kankōkai|pages=41-43}}{{Cite book|和書|author=Moriya Sanae|year=2017|title=The Guide to the Chino Moriya Archive|edition=Third Edition|pages=2-3|editor=Chino Moriya Archive|editor-link=神長官守矢史料館}} It is said that the shrine originally stood in Hanoka Village (now Okaya's Minato district). When residents moved to Hashiwara Village, they brought Moriya Shrine with them.{{Cite book|和書|editor=Hanoka District History Committee|year=1983|title=Hanoka District History|chapter=民間信仰と伝承|pages=620,637}} === The Fuji of the Tenryū River === A legend recounts that Moriya Shrine was once located by the riverbank. Wisteria trees in its precincts grew intertwined with those across the river at Fujishima Shrine, creating a bridge-like canopy. During the Kanbun era (1661–1673), the {{ill|Suwa Domain|ja|諏訪藩}} lord ordered their removal for a firefly-viewing event. However, local villagers feared divine retribution and refused. A man named Koishi Kaemon took on the task for a fee but soon fell into madness and met a tragic end, further cementing the wisteria’s sacred status. == Worship and Rituals == * [[Genshi-sai]] (元始祭) * [[Insect-Sending Festival]] (虫送り祭) * **Annual Festival** (festival): October 20 or the third Sunday in October * [[Ninenmairi]] (二年参り): December 31 * **Onbashira Festival** (御柱祭): Conducted in years of the Tiger and Monkey : Historically aligned with the {{ill|Lower Shrine|ja|諏訪大社#下社}} festival but now held on separate dates. == Access == * 15 minutes (1.1 km) on foot from {{ill|Okaya Station|ja|岡谷駅}}, JR {{ill|Chūō Main Line|ja|中央本線}} * 1 minute from "Moriya Shrine Front" bus stop, {{ill|Silky Bus Kawagishi Line|ja|シルキーバス川岸橋原線}} == Related Sites == === Former Moriya Ōkami Site === Located 300m from the shrine, marked by a stone monument with calligraphy by Moriya Jikken, the 75th shrine chief. === Ryūgen Mountain Deity === A small shrine about 1.3 km from Moriya Shrine, dedicated to {{ill|Ōyama-tsumi|ja|大山祇神}}. The area was once part of Moriya no Kami’s sphere of influence. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://moriyajinja.amebaownd.com/ Official Moriya Shrine Website] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Suwa Faith}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Moriya Shrine}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Okaya, Nagano]] [[Category:Suwa Faith]] [[Category:Former village shrines]] [[Category:History of Okaya, Nagano]] [[Category:Tenryū River]] ==References== {{reflist}} {{Suwa Faith}} [[ja:洩矢神社]][[Category:Suwa faith]] [[Category:Suwa shrines]] [[Category:original pages]] [[en:Morikawa Shrine]]

{{Shrine |name = Moriya Shrine |image = [[File:洩矢神社（岡谷市）- Moriya Shrine (Okaya).jpg|280px]]
Moriya Shrine Torii {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=280|coord={{coord|36|03|04|N|138|02|10|E}}}} |location = {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}, {{ill|Okaya, Nagano|ja|岡谷市}}, Kawagishi Higashi 1-12 |coordinates = {{coord|36|03|04|N|138|02|10|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Moriya no Kami|ja|洩矢神}} |type = {{ill|Village shrine (Japan)|ja|村社}} |創建 = Unknown |alternative names = Moriya Daimyōjin, Moriyama Daimyōjin, Moriya Ōkami, Moriya Shrine |festival = October 20 (Currently the third Sunday in October) }} '''Moriya Shrine''' (読み仮名) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Okaya, Nagano|ja|岡谷市}}, Nagano Prefecture, in the Kawagishi Higashi Hashiwara district (formerly {{ill|Kawagishi Village|ja|川岸村|橋原村}}). The shrine's former classification under the {{ill|modern shrine ranking system|ja|近代type制度}} was that of a {{ill|Village shrine (Japan)|ja|村社}}. == Overview == The shrine enshrines {{ill|Moriya no Kami|ja|洩矢神}}, a deity from {{ill|Suwa|ja|諏訪地方}} traditions. Its exact date of establishment is unknown. The shrine currently stands approximately 200 meters from the {{ill|Tenryū River|ja|天竜川}}, though it was originally said to be located by the riverbank. Archaeological evidence suggests that the area surrounding the shrine has been inhabited since the {{ill|Jōmon period|ja|縄文時代}}. Near the shrine's southwestern side lies the "Kyōzuka (Kyōzukahara) Site" {{Cite book|和書|year=1980|title=長野県中央道埋蔵文化財包蔵地発掘調査報告書 岡谷市その4 昭和52・53年度|editor=長野県中央道遺跡調査団|publisher=[[Japan Highway Public Corporation]] Nagoya Construction Bureau, [[Nagano Prefectural Board of Education]]|chapter=第2節 経塚（経塚原）遺跡|pages=55-96|url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/en/8040}}, once believed to be the dwelling of {{ill|Moriya no Kami|ja|洩矢神}}.{{Cite book|和書|year=1924|title=諏訪史 第1巻|author=Torii Ryūzō|authorlink=Torii Ryūzō|publisher=Shinano Educational Society Suwa Branch|pages=373-374|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/935820/373}} == Deity == * '''{{ill|Moriya no Kami|ja|洩矢神}}''' : The local {{ill|Ujigami|ja|産土神}} of the former Hashiwara Village and a distant ancestor of the {{ill|Moriya clan|ja|守矢氏}}, historically associated with the {{ill|Suwa Taisha Upper Shrine|ja|諏訪大社#上社}}. == History == According to the {{ill|Takeminakata#Mythological entry into Suwa|ja|タケミナカタ#明神入諏|Suwa Myths}}, Moriya no Kami, an indigenous {{ill|Kunitsukami|ja|国津神}}, opposed the advance of {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方神}} (the Suwa deity) into Suwa. Following a battle, Moriya no Kami was defeated and submitted to Takeminakata, becoming his vassal and the progenitor of the {{ill|Moriya clan|ja|守矢氏}}, who served the Suwa Upper Shrine.Miyasaka Mitsuaki, ''Suwa Taisha no Onbashira to Nenchū Gyōji'' (郷土出版社, 1992), pp. 91-93.{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.nagano-jinjacho.jp/shibu/04nansin/02suwa/4030.html|title=Moriya Shrine|website=Nagano Shrine Association|accessdate=2018-10-01}} Medieval texts locate the confrontation between the two deities near {{ill|Mount Moriya|ja|守屋山}}, around the {{ill|Upper Shrine Main Sanctuary|ja|諏訪大社#上社|本宮}} area. However, later Edo-period traditions identify the {{ill|Tenryū River|ja|天竜川}} banks as their battleground. Today, Moriya Shrine and nearby {{ill|Fujishima Shrine (Suwa)|ja|藤島神社 (諏訪地方)}} are recognized as the sites of their respective camps.{{Cite book|和書|author=Imai Nokiku|year=1960|title=Suwa Monogatari|publisher=Kōyō Shobō|pages=3-15|chapter=Moriya no Kami and Takeminakata}}{{Cite book|和書|author=Imai Nokiku|year=1976|title=The Land of the Gods: Ancient Suwa Tales|publisher=Kokusho Kankōkai|pages=41-43}}{{Cite book|和書|author=Moriya Sanae|year=2017|title=The Guide to the Chino Moriya Archive|edition=Third Edition|pages=2-3|editor=Chino Moriya Archive|editor-link=神長官守矢史料館}} It is said that the shrine originally stood in Hanoka Village (now Okaya's Minato district). When residents moved to Hashiwara Village, they brought Moriya Shrine with them.{{Cite book|和書|editor=Hanoka District History Committee|year=1983|title=Hanoka District History|chapter=民間信仰と伝承|pages=620,637}} === The Fuji of the Tenryū River === A legend recounts that Moriya Shrine was once located by the riverbank. Wisteria trees in its precincts grew intertwined with those across the river at Fujishima Shrine, creating a bridge-like canopy. During the Kanbun era (1661–1673), the {{ill|Suwa Domain|ja|諏訪藩}} lord ordered their removal for a firefly-viewing event. However, local villagers feared divine retribution and refused. A man named Koishi Kaemon took on the task for a fee but soon fell into madness and met a tragic end, further cementing the wisteria’s sacred status. == Worship and Rituals == * [[Genshi-sai]] (元始祭) * [[Insect-Sending Festival]] (虫送り祭) * **Annual Festival** (festival): October 20 or the third Sunday in October * [[Ninenmairi]] (二年参り): December 31 * **Onbashira Festival** (御柱祭): Conducted in years of the Tiger and Monkey : Historically aligned with the {{ill|Lower Shrine|ja|諏訪大社#下社}} festival but now held on separate dates. == Access == * 15 minutes (1.1 km) on foot from {{ill|Okaya Station|ja|岡谷駅}}, JR {{ill|Chūō Main Line|ja|中央本線}} * 1 minute from "Moriya Shrine Front" bus stop, {{ill|Silky Bus Kawagishi Line|ja|シルキーバス川岸橋原線}} == Related Sites == === Former Moriya Ōkami Site === Located 300m from the shrine, marked by a stone monument with calligraphy by Moriya Jikken, the 75th shrine chief. === Ryūgen Mountain Deity === A small shrine about 1.3 km from Moriya Shrine, dedicated to {{ill|Ōyama-tsumi|ja|大山祇神}}. The area was once part of Moriya no Kami’s sphere of influence. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://moriyajinja.amebaownd.com/ Official Moriya Shrine Website] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Suwa Faith}}[[Category:Suwa Faith]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Moriya Shrine}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Okaya, Nagano]] [[Category:Suwa Faith]] [[Category:Former village shrines]] [[Category:History of Okaya, Nagano]] [[Category:Tenryū River]] ==References== {{reflist}} {{Suwa Faith}}[[Category:Suwa Faith]] [[ja:洩矢神社]][[Category:Suwa faith]] [[Category:Suwa shrines]] [[Category:original pages]] [[en:Morikawa Shrine]]

Its exact date of establishment is unknown. The shrine currently stands approximately 200 meters from the {{ill|Tenryū River|ja|天竜川}}, though it was originally said to be located by the riverbank. Archaeological evidence suggests that the area surrounding the shrine has been inhabited since the {{ill|Jōmon period|ja|縄文時代}}. Near the shrine's southwestern side lies the "Kyōzuka (Kyōzukahara) Site" {{Cite book|和書|year=1980|title=長野県中央道埋蔵文化財包蔵地発掘調査報告書 岡谷市その4 昭和52・53年度|editor=長野県中央道遺跡調査団|publisher=[[Japan Highway Public Corporation]] Nagoya Construction Bureau, [[Nagano Prefectural Board of Education]]|chapter=第2節 経塚（経塚原）遺跡|pages=55-96|url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/en/8040}}, once believed to be the dwelling of {{ill|Moriya no Kami|ja|洩矢神}}.{{Cite book|和書|year=1924|title=諏訪史 第1巻|author=Torii Ryūzō|authorlink=Torii Ryūzō|publisher=Shinano Educational Society Suwa Branch|pages=373-374|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/935820/373}} == Deity == * '''{{ill|Moriya no Kami|ja|洩矢神}}''' : The local {{ill|Ujigami|ja|産土神}} of the former Hashiwara Village and a distant ancestor of the {{ill|Moriya clan|ja|守矢氏}}, historically associated with the {{ill|Suwa Taisha Upper Shrine|ja|諏訪大社#上社}}. == History == According to the {{ill|Takeminakata#Mythological entry into Suwa|ja|タケミナカタ#明神入諏|Suwa Myths}}, Moriya no Kami, an indigenous {{ill|Kunitsukami|ja|国津神}}, opposed the advance of {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方神}} (the Suwa deity) into Suwa. Following a battle, Moriya no Kami was defeated and submitted to Takeminakata, becoming his vassal and the progenitor of the {{ill|Moriya clan|ja|守矢氏}}, who served the Suwa Upper Shrine.Miyasaka Mitsuaki, ''Suwa Taisha no Onbashira to Nenchū Gyōji'' (郷土出版社, 1992), pp. 91-93.{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.nagano-jinjacho.jp/shibu/04nansin/02suwa/4030.html|title=Moriya Shrine|website=Nagano Shrine Association|accessdate=2018-10-01}} Medieval texts locate the confrontation between the two deities near {{ill|Mount Moriya|ja|守屋山}}, around the {{ill|Upper Shrine Main Sanctuary|ja|諏訪大社#上社|本宮}} area. However, later Edo-period traditions identify the {{ill|Tenryū River|ja|天竜川}} banks as their battleground. Today, Moriya Shrine and nearby {{ill|Fujishima Shrine (Suwa)|ja|藤島神社 (諏訪地方)}} are recognized as the sites of their respective camps.{{Cite book|和書|author=Imai Nokiku|year=1960|title=Suwa Monogatari|publisher=Kōyō Shobō|pages=3-15|chapter=Moriya no Kami and Takeminakata}}{{Cite book|和書|author=Imai Nokiku|year=1976|title=The Land of the Gods: Ancient Suwa Tales|publisher=Kokusho Kankōkai|pages=41-43}}{{Cite book|和書|author=Moriya Sanae|year=2017|title=The Guide to the Chino Moriya Archive|edition=Third Edition|pages=2-3|editor=Chino Moriya Archive|editor-link=神長官守矢史料館}} It is said that the shrine originally stood in Hanoka Village (now Okaya's Minato district). When residents moved to Hashiwara Village, they brought Moriya Shrine with them.{{Cite book|和書|editor=Hanoka District History Committee|year=1983|title=Hanoka District History|chapter=民間信仰と伝承|pages=620,637}} === The Fuji of the Tenryū River === A legend recounts that Moriya Shrine was once located by the riverbank. Wisteria trees in its precincts grew intertwined with those across the river at Fujishima Shrine, creating a bridge-like canopy. During the Kanbun era (1661–1673), the {{ill|Suwa Domain|ja|諏訪藩}} lord ordered their removal for a firefly-viewing event. However, local villagers feared divine retribution and refused. A man named Koishi Kaemon took on the task for a fee but soon fell into madness and met a tragic end, further cementing the wisteria’s sacred status. == Worship and Rituals == * [[Genshi-sai]] (元始祭) * [[Insect-Sending Festival]] (虫送り祭) * **Annual Festival** (festival): October 20 or the third Sunday in October * [[Ninenmairi]] (二年参り): December 31 * **Onbashira Festival** (御柱祭): Conducted in years of the Tiger and Monkey : Historically aligned with the {{ill|Lower Shrine|ja|諏訪大社#下社}} festival but now held on separate dates. == Access == * 15 minutes (1.1 km) on foot from {{ill|Okaya Station|ja|岡谷駅}}, JR {{ill|Chūō Main Line|ja|中央本線}} * 1 minute from "Moriya Shrine Front" bus stop, {{ill|Silky Bus Kawagishi Line|ja|シルキーバス川岸橋原線}} == Related Sites == === Former Moriya Ōkami Site === Located 300m from the shrine, marked by a stone monument with calligraphy by Moriya Jikken, the 75th shrine chief. === Ryūgen Mountain Deity === A small shrine about 1.3 km from Moriya Shrine, dedicated to {{ill|Ōyama-tsumi|ja|大山祇神}}. The area was once part of Moriya no Kami’s sphere of influence. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://moriyajinja.amebaownd.com/ Official Moriya Shrine Website] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Suwa Faith}}[[Category:Suwa Faith]][[Category:Suwa Faith]][[Category:Suwa Faith]][[Category:Suwa Faith]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Moriya Shrine}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Okaya, Nagano]] [[Category:Suwa Faith]] [[Category:Former village shrines]] [[Category:History of Okaya, Nagano]] [[Category:Tenryū River]] ==References== {{reflist}} {{Suwa Faith}}[[Category:Suwa Faith]][[Category:Suwa Faith]][[Category:Suwa Faith]][[Category:Suwa Faith]] [[ja:洩矢神社]][[Category:Suwa faith]] [[Category:Suwa shrines]] [[Category:original pages]] [[en:Morikawa Shrine]]

However, later Edo-period traditions identify the {{ill|Tenryū River|ja|天竜川}} banks as their battleground. Today, Moriya Shrine and nearby {{ill|Fujishima Shrine (Suwa)|ja|藤島神社 (諏訪地方)}} are recognized as the sites of their respective camps.{{Cite book|和書|author=Imai Nokiku|year=1960|title=Suwa Monogatari|publisher=Kōyō Shobō|pages=3-15|chapter=Moriya no Kami and Takeminakata}}{{Cite book|和書|author=Imai Nokiku|year=1976|title=The Land of the Gods: Ancient Suwa Tales|publisher=Kokusho Kankōkai|pages=41-43}}{{Cite book|和書|author=Moriya Sanae|year=2017|title=The Guide to the Chino Moriya Archive|edition=Third Edition|pages=2-3|editor=Chino Moriya Archive|editor-link=神長官守矢史料館}} It is said that the shrine originally stood in Hanoka Village (now Okaya's Minato district). Following a battle, Moriya no Kami was defeated and submitted to Takeminakata, becoming his vassal and the progenitor of the {{ill|Moriya clan|ja|守矢氏}}, who served the Suwa Upper Shrine.Miyasaka Mitsuaki, ''Suwa Taisha no Onbashira to Nenchū Gyōji'' (郷土出版社, 1992), pp. 91-93.{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.nagano-jinjacho.jp/shibu/04nansin/02suwa/4030.html|title=Moriya Shrine|website=Nagano Shrine Association|accessdate=2018-10-01}} Medieval texts locate the confrontation between the two deities near {{ill|Mount Moriya|ja|守屋山}}, around the {{ill|Upper Shrine Main Sanctuary|ja|諏訪大社#上社|本宮}} area. However, later Edo-period traditions identify the {{ill|Tenryū River|ja|天竜川}} banks as their battleground. Today, Moriya Shrine and nearby {{ill|Fujishima Shrine (Suwa)|ja|藤島神社 (諏訪地方)}} are recognized as the sites of their respective camps.{{Cite book|和書|author=Imai Nokiku|year=1960|title=Suwa Monogatari|publisher=Kōyō Shobō|pages=3-15|chapter=Moriya no Kami and Takeminakata}}{{Cite book|和書|author=Imai Nokiku|year=1976|title=The Land of the Gods: Ancient Suwa Tales|publisher=Kokusho Kankōkai|pages=41-43}}{{Cite book|和書|author=Moriya Sanae|year=2017|title=The Guide to the Chino Moriya Archive|edition=Third Edition|pages=2-3|editor=Chino Moriya Archive|editor-link=神長官守矢史料館}} It is said that the shrine originally stood in Hanoka Village (now Okaya's Minato district). When residents moved to Hashiwara Village, they brought Moriya Shrine with them.{{Cite book|和書|editor=Hanoka District History Committee|year=1983|title=Hanoka District History|chapter=民間信仰と伝承|pages=620,637}} === The Fuji of the Tenryū River === A legend recounts that Moriya Shrine was once located by the riverbank. Wisteria trees in its precincts grew intertwined with those across the river at Fujishima Shrine, creating a bridge-like canopy. During the Kanbun era (1661–1673), the {{ill|Suwa Domain|ja|諏訪藩}} lord ordered their removal for a firefly-viewing event. However, local villagers feared divine retribution and refused. A man named Koishi Kaemon took on the task for a fee but soon fell into madness and met a tragic end, further cementing the wisteria’s sacred status. == Worship and Rituals == * [[Genshi-sai]] (元始祭) * [[Insect-Sending Festival]] (虫送り祭) * **Annual Festival** (festival): October 20 or the third Sunday in October * [[Ninenmairi]] (二年参り): December 31 * **Onbashira Festival** (御柱祭): Conducted in years of the Tiger and Monkey : Historically aligned with the {{ill|Lower Shrine|ja|諏訪大社#下社}} festival but now held on separate dates. == Access == * 15 minutes (1.1 km) on foot from {{ill|Okaya Station|ja|岡谷駅}}, JR {{ill|Chūō Main Line|ja|中央本線}} * 1 minute from "Moriya Shrine Front" bus stop, {{ill|Silky Bus Kawagishi Line|ja|シルキーバス川岸橋原線}} == Related Sites == === Former Moriya Ōkami Site === Located 300m from the shrine, marked by a stone monument with calligraphy by Moriya Jikken, the 75th shrine chief. === Ryūgen Mountain Deity === A small shrine about 1.3 km from Moriya Shrine, dedicated to {{ill|Ōyama-tsumi|ja|大山祇神}}. The area was once part of Moriya no Kami’s sphere of influence. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://moriyajinja.amebaownd.com/ Official Moriya Shrine Website] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Suwa Faith}}{{DEFAULTSORT:Moriya Shrine}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Okaya, Nagano]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:History of Okaya, Nagano]] [[Category:Tenryū River]] ==References== {{reflist}} {{Suwa Faith}}[[ja:洩矢神社]][[Category:Suwa faith]] [[Category:Suwa shrines]] [[Category:original pages]] [[en:Morikawa Shrine]]

{{infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Moriya Shrine |image = [[File:洩矢神社（岡谷市）- Moriya Shrine (Okaya).jpg|280px]]
Moriya Shrine Torii {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=280|coord={{coord|36|03|04|N|138|02|10|E}}}} |location = {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}, {{ill|Okaya, Nagano|ja|岡谷市}}, Kawagishi Higashi 1-12 |coordinates = {{coord|36|03|04|N|138|02|10|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Moriya no Kami|ja|洩矢神}} |type = {{ill|Village shrine (Japan)|ja|村社}} |創建 = Unknown |alternative names = Moriya Daimyōjin, Moriyama Daimyōjin, Moriya Ōkami, Moriya Shrine |festival = October 20 (Currently the third Sunday in October) }} '''Moriya Shrine''' (読み仮名) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Okaya, Nagano|ja|岡谷市}}, Nagano Prefecture, in the Kawagishi Higashi Hashiwara district (formerly {{ill|Kawagishi Village|ja|川岸村|橋原村}}). The shrine's former classification under the {{ill|modern shrine ranking system|ja|近代type制度}} was that of a {{ill|Village shrine (Japan)|ja|村社}}. == Overview == The shrine enshrines {{ill|Moriya no Kami|ja|洩矢神}}, a deity from {{ill|Suwa|ja|諏訪地方}} traditions. Its exact date of establishment is unknown. The shrine currently stands approximately 200 meters from the {{ill|Tenryū River|ja|天竜川}}, though it was originally said to be located by the riverbank. Archaeological evidence suggests that the area surrounding the shrine has been inhabited since the {{ill|Jōmon period|ja|縄文時代}}. Near the shrine's southwestern side lies the "Kyōzuka (Kyōzukahara) Site" {{Cite book|和書|year=1980|title=長野県中央道埋蔵文化財包蔵地発掘調査報告書 岡谷市その4 昭和52・53年度|editor=長野県中央道遺跡調査団|publisher=[[Japan Highway Public Corporation]] Nagoya Construction Bureau, [[Nagano Prefectural Board of Education]]|chapter=第2節 経塚（経塚原）遺跡|pages=55-96|url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/en/8040}}, once believed to be the dwelling of {{ill|Moriya no Kami|ja|洩矢神}}.{{Cite book|和書|year=1924|title=諏訪史 第1巻|author=Torii Ryūzō|authorlink=Torii Ryūzō|publisher=Shinano Educational Society Suwa Branch|pages=373-374|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/935820/373}} == Deity == * '''{{ill|Moriya no Kami|ja|洩矢神}}''' : The local {{ill|Ujigami|ja|産土神}} of the former Hashiwara Village and a distant ancestor of the {{ill|Moriya clan|ja|守矢氏}}, historically associated with the {{ill|Suwa Taisha Upper Shrine|ja|諏訪大社#上社}}. == History == According to the {{ill|Takeminakata#Mythological entry into Suwa|ja|タケミナカタ#明神入諏|Suwa Myths}}, Moriya no Kami, an indigenous {{ill|Kunitsukami|ja|国津神}}, opposed the advance of {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方神}} (the Suwa deity) into Suwa. Following a battle, Moriya no Kami was defeated and submitted to Takeminakata, becoming his vassal and the progenitor of the {{ill|Moriya clan|ja|守矢氏}}, who served the Suwa Upper Shrine.Miyasaka Mitsuaki, ''Suwa Taisha no Onbashira to Nenchū Gyōji'' (郷土出版社, 1992), pp. 91-93.{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.nagano-jinjacho.jp/shibu/04nansin/02suwa/4030.html|title=Moriya Shrine|website=Nagano Shrine Association|accessdate=2018-10-01}} Medieval texts locate the confrontation between the two deities near {{ill|Mount Moriya|ja|守屋山}}, around the {{ill|Upper Shrine Main Sanctuary|ja|諏訪大社#上社|本宮}} area. However, later Edo-period traditions identify the {{ill|Tenryū River|ja|天竜川}} banks as their battleground. Today, Moriya Shrine and nearby {{ill|Fujishima Shrine (Suwa)|ja|藤島神社 (諏訪地方)}} are recognized as the sites of their respective camps.{{Cite book|和書|author=Imai Nokiku|year=1960|title=Suwa Monogatari|publisher=Kōyō Shobō|pages=3-15|chapter=Moriya no Kami and Takeminakata}}{{Cite book|和書|author=Imai Nokiku|year=1976|title=The Land of the Gods: Ancient Suwa Tales|publisher=Kokusho Kankōkai|pages=41-43}}{{Cite book|和書|author=Moriya Sanae|year=2017|title=The Guide to the Chino Moriya Archive|edition=Third Edition|pages=2-3|editor=Chino Moriya Archive|editor-link=神長官守矢史料館}} It is said that the shrine originally stood in Hanoka Village (now Okaya's Minato district). When residents moved to Hashiwara Village, they brought Moriya Shrine with them.{{Cite book|和書|editor=Hanoka District History Committee|year=1983|title=Hanoka District History|chapter=民間信仰と伝承|pages=620,637}} === The Fuji of the Tenryū River === A legend recounts that Moriya Shrine was once located by the riverbank. Wisteria trees in its precincts grew intertwined with those across the river at Fujishima Shrine, creating a bridge-like canopy. During the Kanbun era (1661–1673), the {{ill|Suwa Domain|ja|諏訪藩}} lord ordered their removal for a firefly-viewing event. However, local villagers feared divine retribution and refused. A man named Koishi Kaemon took on the task for a fee but soon fell into madness and met a tragic end, further cementing the wisteria’s sacred status. == Worship and Rituals == * [[Genshi-sai]] (元始祭) * [[Insect-Sending Festival]] (虫送り祭) * **Annual Festival** (festival): October 20 or the third Sunday in October * [[Ninenmairi]] (二年参り): December 31 * **Onbashira Festival** (御柱祭): Conducted in years of the Tiger and Monkey : Historically aligned with the {{ill|Lower Shrine|ja|諏訪大社#下社}} festival but now held on separate dates. == Access == * 15 minutes (1.1 km) on foot from {{ill|Okaya Station|ja|岡谷駅}}, JR {{ill|Chūō Main Line|ja|中央本線}} * 1 minute from "Moriya Shrine Front" bus stop, {{ill|Silky Bus Kawagishi Line|ja|シルキーバス川岸橋原線}} == Related Sites == === Former Moriya Ōkami Site === Located 300m from the shrine, marked by a stone monument with calligraphy by Moriya Jikken, the 75th shrine chief. === Ryūgen Mountain Deity === A small shrine about 1.3 km from Moriya Shrine, dedicated to {{ill|Ōyama-tsumi|ja|大山祇神}}. The area was once part of Moriya no Kami’s sphere of influence. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://moriyajinja.amebaownd.com/ Official Moriya Shrine Website] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Suwa Faith}}{{DEFAULTSORT:Moriya Shrine}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Okaya, Nagano]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:History of Okaya, Nagano]] [[Category:Tenryū River]] ==References== {{reflist}} {{Suwa Faith}}[[ja:洩矢神社]][[Category:Suwa faith]] [[Category:Suwa shrines]] [[Category:original pages]] [[en:Morikawa Shrine]]

A man named Koishi Kaemon took on the task for a fee but soon fell into madness and met a tragic end, further cementing the wisteria’s sacred status. == Worship and Rituals == * [[Genshi-sai]] (元始祭) * [[Insect-Sending Festival]] (虫送り祭) * '''Annual Festival''' (festival): October 20 or the third Sunday in October * [[Ninenmairi]] (二年参り): December 31 * '''Onbashira Festival''' (御柱祭): Conducted in years of the Tiger and Monkey : Historically aligned with the {{ill|Lower Shrine|ja|諏訪大社#下社}} festival but now held on separate dates. == Access == * 15 minutes (1.1 km) on foot from {{ill|Okaya Station|ja|岡谷駅}}, JR {{ill|Chūō Main Line|ja|中央本線}} * 1 minute from "Moriya Shrine Front" bus stop, {{ill|Silky Bus Kawagishi Line|ja|シルキーバス川岸橋原線}} == Related Sites == === Former Moriya Ōkami Site === Located 300m from the shrine, marked by a stone monument with calligraphy by Moriya Jikken, the 75th shrine chief. === Ryūgen Mountain Deity === A small shrine about 1.3 km from Moriya Shrine, dedicated to {{ill|Ōyama-tsumi|ja|大山祇神}}. The area was once part of Moriya no Kami’s sphere of influence. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://moriyajinja.amebaownd.com/ Official Moriya Shrine Website] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Suwa Faith}}{{DEFAULTSORT:Moriya Shrine}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Okaya, Nagano]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:History of Okaya, Nagano]] [[Category:Tenryū River]] ==References== {{reflist}} {{Suwa Faith}}[[ja:洩矢神社]][[Category:Suwa faith]] [[Category:Suwa shrines]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:New pages]] [[en:Morikawa Shrine]]

revision.comment: deciding to rename this category to a more descriptive one
revision.text:
{{infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Moriya Shrine |image = [[File:洩矢神社（岡谷市）- Moriya Shrine (Okaya).jpg|280px]]
Moriya Shrine Torii {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=280|coord={{coord|36|03|04|N|138|02|10|E}}}} |location = {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}, {{ill|Okaya, Nagano|ja|岡谷市}}, Kawagishi Higashi 1-12 |coordinates = {{coord|36|03|04|N|138|02|10|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Moriya no Kami|ja|洩矢神}} |type = {{ill|Village shrine (Japan)|ja|村社}} |創建 = Unknown |alternative names = Moriya Daimyōjin, Moriyama Daimyōjin, Moriya Ōkami, Moriya Shrine |festival = October 20 (Currently the third Sunday in October) }} '''Moriya Shrine''' (読み仮名) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Okaya, Nagano|ja|岡谷市}}, Nagano Prefecture, in the Kawagishi Higashi Hashiwara district (formerly {{ill|Kawagishi Village|ja|川岸村|橋原村}}). The shrine's former classification under the {{ill|modern shrine ranking system|ja|近代type制度}} was that of a {{ill|Village shrine (Japan)|ja|村社}}. == Overview == The shrine enshrines {{ill|Moriya no Kami|ja|洩矢神}}, a deity from {{ill|Suwa|ja|諏訪地方}} traditions. Its exact date of establishment is unknown. The shrine currently stands approximately 200 meters from the {{ill|Tenryū River|ja|天竜川}}, though it was originally said to be located by the riverbank. Archaeological evidence suggests that the area surrounding the shrine has been inhabited since the {{ill|Jōmon period|ja|縄文時代}}. Near the shrine's southwestern side lies the "Kyōzuka (Kyōzukahara) Site" {{Cite book|和書|year=1980|title=長野県中央道埋蔵文化財包蔵地発掘調査報告書 岡谷市その4 昭和52・53年度|editor=長野県中央道遺跡調査団|publisher=[[Japan Highway Public Corporation]] Nagoya Construction Bureau, [[Nagano Prefectural Board of Education]]|chapter=第2節 経塚（経塚原）遺跡|pages=55-96|url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/en/8040}}, once believed to be the dwelling of {{ill|Moriya no Kami|ja|洩矢神}}.{{Cite book|和書|year=1924|title=諏訪史 第1巻|author=Torii Ryūzō|authorlink=Torii Ryūzō|publisher=Shinano Educational Society Suwa Branch|pages=373-374|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/935820/373}} == Deity == * '''{{ill|Moriya no Kami|ja|洩矢神}}''' : The local {{ill|Ujigami|ja|産土神}} of the former Hashiwara Village and a distant ancestor of the {{ill|Moriya clan|ja|守矢氏}}, historically associated with the {{ill|Suwa Taisha Upper Shrine|ja|諏訪大社#上社}}. == History == According to the {{ill|Takeminakata#Mythological entry into Suwa|ja|タケミナカタ#明神入諏|Suwa Myths}}, Moriya no Kami, an indigenous {{ill|Kunitsukami|ja|国津神}}, opposed the advance of {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方神}} (the Suwa deity) into Suwa. Following a battle, Moriya no Kami was defeated and submitted to Takeminakata, becoming his vassal and the progenitor of the {{ill|Moriya clan|ja|守矢氏}}, who served the Suwa Upper Shrine.Miyasaka Mitsuaki, ''Suwa Taisha no Onbashira to Nenchū Gyōji'' (郷土出版社, 1992), pp. 91-93.{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.nagano-jinjacho.jp/shibu/04nansin/02suwa/4030.html|title=Moriya Shrine|website=Nagano Shrine Association|accessdate=2018-10-01}} Medieval texts locate the confrontation between the two deities near {{ill|Mount Moriya|ja|守屋山}}, around the {{ill|Upper Shrine Main Sanctuary|ja|諏訪大社#上社|本宮}} area. However, later Edo-period traditions identify the {{ill|Tenryū River|ja|天竜川}} banks as their battleground. Today, Moriya Shrine and nearby {{ill|Fujishima Shrine (Suwa)|ja|藤島神社 (諏訪地方)}} are recognized as the sites of their respective camps.{{Cite book|和書|author=Imai Nokiku|year=1960|title=Suwa Monogatari|publisher=Kōyō Shobō|pages=3-15|chapter=Moriya no Kami and Takeminakata}}{{Cite book|和書|author=Imai Nokiku|year=1976|title=The Land of the Gods: Ancient Suwa Tales|publisher=Kokusho Kankōkai|pages=41-43}}{{Cite book|和書|author=Moriya Sanae|year=2017|title=The Guide to the Chino Moriya Archive|edition=Third Edition|pages=2-3|editor=Chino Moriya Archive|editor-link=神長官守矢史料館}} It is said that the shrine originally stood in Hanoka Village (now Okaya's Minato district). When residents moved to Hashiwara Village, they brought Moriya Shrine with them.{{Cite book|和書|editor=Hanoka District History Committee|year=1983|title=Hanoka District History|chapter=民間信仰と伝承|pages=620,637}} === The Fuji of the Tenryū River === A legend recounts that Moriya Shrine was once located by the riverbank. Wisteria trees in its precincts grew intertwined with those across the river at Fujishima Shrine, creating a bridge-like canopy. During the Kanbun era (1661–1673), the {{ill|Suwa Domain|ja|諏訪藩}} lord ordered their removal for a firefly-viewing event. However, local villagers feared divine retribution and refused. A man named Koishi Kaemon took on the task for a fee but soon fell into madness and met a tragic end, further cementing the wisteria’s sacred status. == Worship and Rituals == * [[Genshi-sai]] (元始祭) * [[Insect-Sending Festival]] (虫送り祭) * '''Annual Festival''' (festival): October 20 or the third Sunday in October * [[Ninenmairi]] (二年参り): December 31 * '''Onbashira Festival''' (御柱祭): Conducted in years of the Tiger and Monkey : Historically aligned with the {{ill|Lower Shrine|ja|諏訪大社#下社}} festival but now held on separate dates. == Access == * 15 minutes (1.1 km) on foot from {{ill|Okaya Station|ja|岡谷駅}}, JR {{ill|Chūō Main Line|ja|中央本線}} * 1 minute from "Moriya Shrine Front" bus stop, {{ill|Silky Bus Kawagishi Line|ja|シルキーバス川岸橋原線}} == Related Sites == === Former Moriya Ōkami Site === Located 300m from the shrine, marked by a stone monument with calligraphy by Moriya Jikken, the 75th shrine chief. === Ryūgen Mountain Deity === A small shrine about 1.3 km from Moriya Shrine, dedicated to {{ill|Ōyama-tsumi|ja|大山祇神}}. The area was once part of Moriya no Kami’s sphere of influence. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://moriyajinja.amebaownd.com/ Official Moriya Shrine Website] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Suwa Faith}}{{DEFAULTSORT:Moriya Shrine}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Okaya, Nagano]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:History of Okaya, Nagano]] [[Category:Tenryū River]] ==References== {{reflist}} {{Suwa Faith}}[[ja:洩矢神社]][[Category:Suwa faith]] [[Category:Suwa shrines]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[en:Morikawa Shrine]]

Moriya Shrine Torii {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=280|coord={{coord|36|03|04|N|138|02|10|E}}}} |location = {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}, {{ill|Okaya, Nagano|ja|岡谷市}}, Kawagishi Higashi 1-12 |coordinates = {{coord|36|03|04|N|138|02|10|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Moriya no Kami|ja|洩矢神}} |type = {{ill|Village shrine (Japan)|ja|村社}} |創建 = Unknown |alternative names = Moriya Daimyōjin, Moriyama Daimyōjin, Moriya Ōkami, Moriya Shrine |festival = October 20 (Currently the third Sunday in October) }} '''Moriya Shrine''' (読み仮名) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Okaya, Nagano|ja|岡谷市}}, Nagano Prefecture, in the Kawagishi Higashi Hashiwara district (formerly {{ill|Kawagishi Village|ja|川岸村|橋原村}}). The shrine's former classification under the {{ill|modern shrine ranking system|ja|近代type制度}} was that of a {{ill|Village shrine (Japan)|ja|村社}}. == Overview == The shrine enshrines {{ill|Moriya no Kami|ja|洩矢神}}, a deity from {{ill|Suwa|ja|諏訪地方}} traditions. Its exact date of establishment is unknown. The shrine currently stands approximately 200 meters from the {{ill|Tenryū River|ja|天竜川}}, though it was originally said to be located by the riverbank. Archaeological evidence suggests that the area surrounding the shrine has been inhabited since the {{ill|Jōmon period|ja|縄文時代}}. Near the shrine's southwestern side lies the "Kyōzuka (Kyōzukahara) Site" {{Cite book|和書|year=1980|title=長野県中央道埋蔵文化財包蔵地発掘調査報告書 岡谷市その4 昭和52・53年度|editor=長野県中央道遺跡調査団|publisher=[[Japan Highway Public Corporation]] Nagoya Construction Bureau, [[Nagano Prefectural Board of Education]]|chapter=第2節 経塚（経塚原）遺跡|pages=55-96|url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/en/8040}}, once believed to be the dwelling of {{ill|Moriya no Kami|ja|洩矢神}}.{{Cite book|和書|year=1924|title=諏訪史 第1巻|author=Torii Ryūzō|authorlink=Torii Ryūzō|publisher=Shinano Educational Society Suwa Branch|pages=373-374|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/935820/373}} == deity == * '''{{ill|Moriya no Kami|ja|洩矢神}}''' : The local {{ill|Ujigami|ja|産土神}} of the former Hashiwara Village and a distant ancestor of the {{ill|Moriya clan|ja|守矢氏}}, historically associated with the {{ill|Suwa Taisha Upper Shrine|ja|諏訪大社#上社}}. == History == According to the {{ill|Takeminakata#Mythological entry into Suwa|ja|タケミナカタ#明神入諏|Suwa Myths}}, Moriya no Kami, an indigenous {{ill|Kunitsukami|ja|国津神}}, opposed the advance of {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方神}} (the Suwa deity) into Suwa. Following a battle, Moriya no Kami was defeated and submitted to Takeminakata, becoming his vassal and the progenitor of the {{ill|Moriya clan|ja|守矢氏}}, who served the Suwa Upper Shrine.Miyasaka Mitsuaki, ''Suwa Taisha no Onbashira to Nenchū Gyōji'' (郷土出版社, 1992), pp. 91-93.{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.nagano-jinjacho.jp/shibu/04nansin/02suwa/4030.html|title=Moriya Shrine|website=Nagano Shrine Association|accessdate=2018-10-01}} Medieval texts locate the confrontation between the two deities near {{ill|Mount Moriya|ja|守屋山}}, around the {{ill|Upper Shrine Main Sanctuary|ja|諏訪大社#上社|本宮}} area. However, later Edo-period traditions identify the {{ill|Tenryū River|ja|天竜川}} banks as their battleground. Today, Moriya Shrine and nearby {{ill|Fujishima Shrine (Suwa)|ja|藤島神社 (諏訪地方)}} are recognized as the sites of their respective camps.{{Cite book|和書|author=Imai Nokiku|year=1960|title=Suwa Monogatari|publisher=Kōyō Shobō|pages=3-15|chapter=Moriya no Kami and Takeminakata}}{{Cite book|和書|author=Imai Nokiku|year=1976|title=The Land of the Gods: Ancient Suwa Tales|publisher=Kokusho Kankōkai|pages=41-43}}{{Cite book|和書|author=Moriya Sanae|year=2017|title=The Guide to the Chino Moriya Archive|edition=Third Edition|pages=2-3|editor=Chino Moriya Archive|editor-link=神長官守矢史料館}} It is said that the shrine originally stood in Hanoka Village (now Okaya's Minato district). When residents moved to Hashiwara Village, they brought Moriya Shrine with them.{{Cite book|和書|editor=Hanoka District History Committee|year=1983|title=Hanoka District History|chapter=民間信仰と伝承|pages=620,637}} === The Fuji of the Tenryū River === A legend recounts that Moriya Shrine was once located by the riverbank. Wisteria trees in its precincts grew intertwined with those across the river at Fujishima Shrine, creating a bridge-like canopy. During the Kanbun era (1661–1673), the {{ill|Suwa Domain|ja|諏訪藩}} lord ordered their removal for a firefly-viewing event. However, local villagers feared divine retribution and refused. A man named Koishi Kaemon took on the task for a fee but soon fell into madness and met a tragic end, further cementing the wisteria’s sacred status. == Worship and Rituals == * [[Genshi-sai]] (元始祭) * {{ill|Insect-Sending Festival|ja|虫送り}} (虫送り祭) * '''Annual Festival''' (festival): October 20 or the third Sunday in October * [[Ninenmairi]] (二年参り): December 31 * '''Onbashira Festival''' (御柱祭): Conducted in years of the Tiger and Monkey : Historically aligned with the {{ill|Lower Shrine|ja|諏訪大社#下社}} festival but now held on separate dates. == Access == * 15 minutes (1.1 km) on foot from {{ill|Okaya Station|ja|岡谷駅}}, JR {{ill|Chūō Main Line|ja|中央本線}} * 1 minute from "Moriya Shrine Front" bus stop, {{ill|Silky Bus Kawagishi Line|ja|シルキーバス川岸橋原線}} == Related Sites == === Former Moriya Ōkami Site === Located 300m from the shrine, marked by a stone monument with calligraphy by Moriya Jikken, the 75th shrine chief. === Ryūgen Mountain deity === A small shrine about 1.3 km from Moriya Shrine, dedicated to {{ill|Ōyama-tsumi|ja|大山祇神}}. The area was once part of Moriya no Kami’s sphere of influence. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://moriyajinja.amebaownd.com/ Official Moriya Shrine Website] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Suwa Faith}}{{DEFAULTSORT:Moriya Shrine}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Okaya, Nagano]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:History of Okaya, Nagano]] [[Category:Tenryū River]] ==References== {{reflist}} {{Suwa Faith}}[[ja:洩矢神社]][[Category:Suwa faith]] [[Category:Suwa shrines]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:original pages]] [[en:Morikawa Shrine]]

Its exact date of establishment is unknown. The shrine currently stands approximately 200 meters from the {{ill|Tenryū River|ja|天竜川}}, though it was originally said to be located by the riverbank. Archaeological evidence suggests that the area surrounding the shrine has been inhabited since the {{ill|Jōmon period|ja|縄文時代}}. Near the shrine's southwestern side lies the "Kyōzuka (Kyōzukahara) Site" {{Cite book|和書|year=1980|title=長野県中央道埋蔵文化財包蔵地発掘調査報告書 岡谷市その4 昭和52・53年度|editor=長野県中央道遺跡調査団|publisher=[[Japan Highway Public Corporation]] Nagoya Construction Bureau, [[Nagano Prefectural Board of Education]]|chapter=第2節 経塚（経塚原）遺跡|pages=55-96|url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/en/8040}}, once believed to be the dwelling of {{ill|Moriya no Kami|ja|洩矢神}}.{{Cite book|和書|year=1924|title=諏訪史 第1巻|author=Torii Ryūzō|authorlink=Torii Ryūzō|publisher=Shinano Educational Society Suwa Branch|pages=373-374|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/935820/373}} == deity == * '''{{ill|Moriya no Kami|ja|洩矢神}}''' : The local {{ill|Ujigami|ja|産土神}} of the former Hashiwara Village and a distant ancestor of the {{ill|Moriya clan|ja|守矢氏}}, historically associated with the {{ill|Suwa Taisha Upper Shrine|ja|諏訪大社#上社}}. == History == According to the {{ill|Takeminakata#Mythological entry into Suwa|ja|タケミナカタ#明神入諏|Suwa Myths}}, Moriya no Kami, an indigenous {{ill|Kunitsukami|ja|国津神}}, opposed the advance of {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方神}} (the Suwa deity) into Suwa. Following a battle, Moriya no Kami was defeated and submitted to Takeminakata, becoming his vassal and the progenitor of the {{ill|Moriya clan|ja|守矢氏}}, who served the Suwa Upper Shrine.Miyasaka Mitsuaki, ''Suwa Taisha no Onbashira to Nenchū Gyōji'' (郷土出版社, 1992), pp. 91-93.{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.nagano-jinjacho.jp/shibu/04nansin/02suwa/4030.html|title=Moriya Shrine|website=Nagano Shrine Association|accessdate=2018-10-01}} Medieval texts locate the confrontation between the two deities near {{ill|Mount Moriya|ja|守屋山}}, around the {{ill|Upper Shrine Main Sanctuary|ja|諏訪大社#上社|本宮}} area. However, later Edo-period traditions identify the {{ill|Tenryū River|ja|天竜川}} banks as their battleground. Today, Moriya Shrine and nearby {{ill|Fujishima Shrine (Suwa)|ja|藤島神社 (諏訪地方)}} are recognized as the sites of their respective camps.{{Cite book|和書|author=Imai Nokiku|year=1960|title=Suwa Monogatari|publisher=Kōyō Shobō|pages=3-15|chapter=Moriya no Kami and Takeminakata}}{{Cite book|和書|author=Imai Nokiku|year=1976|title=The Land of the Gods: Ancient Suwa Tales|publisher=Kokusho Kankōkai|pages=41-43}}{{Cite book|和書|author=Moriya Sanae|year=2017|title=The Guide to the Chino Moriya Archive|edition=Third Edition|pages=2-3|editor=Chino Moriya Archive|editor-link=神長官守矢史料館}} It is said that the shrine originally stood in Hanoka Village (now Okaya's Minato district). When residents moved to Hashiwara Village, they brought Moriya Shrine with them.{{Cite book|和書|editor=Hanoka District History Committee|year=1983|title=Hanoka District History|chapter=民間信仰と伝承|pages=620,637}} === The Fuji of the Tenryū River === A legend recounts that Moriya Shrine was once located by the riverbank. Wisteria trees in its precincts grew intertwined with those across the river at Fujishima Shrine, creating a bridge-like canopy. During the Kanbun era (1661–1673), the {{ill|Suwa Domain|ja|諏訪藩}} lord ordered their removal for a firefly-viewing event. However, local villagers feared divine retribution and refused. A man named Koishi Kaemon took on the task for a fee but soon fell into madness and met a tragic end, further cementing the wisteria’s sacred status. == Worship and Rituals == * [[Genshi-sai]] (元始祭) * {{ill|Insect-Sending Festival|ja|虫送り}} (虫送り祭) * '''Annual Festival''' (festival): October 20 or the third Sunday in October * [[Ninenmairi]] (二年参り): December 31 * '''Onbashira Festival''' (御柱祭): Conducted in years of the Tiger and Monkey : Historically aligned with the {{ill|Lower Shrine|ja|諏訪大社#下社}} festival but now held on separate dates. == Access == * 15 minutes (1.1 km) on foot from {{ill|Okaya Station|ja|岡谷駅}}, JR {{ill|Chūō Main Line|ja|中央本線}} * 1 minute from "Moriya Shrine Front" bus stop, {{ill|Silky Bus Kawagishi Line|ja|シルキーバス川岸橋原線}} == Related Sites == === Former Moriya Ōkami Site === Located 300m from the shrine, marked by a stone monument with calligraphy by Moriya Jikken, the 75th shrine chief. === Ryūgen Mountain deity === A small shrine about 1.3 km from Moriya Shrine, dedicated to {{ill|Ōyama-tsumi|ja|大山祇神}}. The area was once part of Moriya no Kami’s sphere of influence. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [https://moriyajinja.amebaownd.com/ Official Moriya Shrine Website] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Suwa Faith}}{{DEFAULTSORT:Moriya Shrine}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagano Prefecture]] [[Category:Okaya, Nagano]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:History of Okaya, Nagano]] [[Category:Tenryū River]] ==References== {{reflist}} {{Suwa Faith}}[[ja:洩矢神社]][[Category:Suwa faith]] [[Category:Suwa shrines]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Moriya Shrine]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Moriya Shrine]][[Category:kk]] [[en:Morikawa Shrine]]

{{infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Moriya Shrine |image = [[File:洩矢神社（岡谷市）- Moriya Shrine (Okaya).jpg|280px]]
Moriya Shrine Torii {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=280|coord={{coord|36|03|04|N|138|02|10|E}}}} |location = {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|ja|長野県}}, {{ill|Okaya, Nagano|ja|岡谷市}}, Kawagishi Higashi 1-12 |coordinates = {{coord|36|03|04|N|138|02|10|E|region:JP|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Moriya no Kami|ja|洩矢神}} |type = {{ill|Village shrine (Japan)|ja|村社}} |創建 = Unknown |alternative names = Moriya Daimyōjin, Moriyama Daimyōjin, Moriya Ōkami, Moriya Shrine |festival = October 20 (Currently the third Sunday in October) }} '''Moriya Shrine''' (読み仮名) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Okaya, Nagano|ja|岡谷市}}, Nagano Prefecture, in the Kawagishi Higashi Hashiwara district (formerly {{ill|Kawagishi Village|ja|川岸村|橋原村}}). Its exact date of establishment is unknown. The shrine currently stands approximately 200 meters from the {{ill|Tenryū River|ja|天竜川}}, though it was originally said to be located by the riverbank. Archaeological evidence suggests that the area surrounding the shrine has been inhabited since the {{ill|Jōmon period|ja|縄文時代}}.
