{{infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Moriya Shrine |image = File:Moriya Shrine - 洩矢神社 社殿.jpg |location = {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|en|Nagano Prefecture|qid=Q127877|lt=Nagano Prefecture|1=Nagano Prefecture}}, {{ill|Okaya, Nagano|en|Okaya, Nagano|qid=Q838672|lt=Okaya, Nagano|1=Okaya, Nagano}}, Kawagishi Higashi 1-12 |coordinates = {{Coord|36.051111111111|N|138.03611111111|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Moreya|qid=Q11146526|1=Moreya}} |type = {{ill|Village shrine (Japan)|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|qid=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine (Japan)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q712534}} |創建 = Unknown |alternative names = Moriya Daimyōjin, Moriyama Daimyōjin, Moriya Ōkami, Moriya Shrine |festival = October 20 (Currently the third Sunday in October) |prefecture = {{ill|Okaya|qid=Q838672|1=Okaya, Nagano}} }}
'''Moriya Shrine''' (読み仮名) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Okaya, Nagano|en|Okaya, Nagano|qid=Q838672|lt=Okaya, Nagano|1=Okaya, Nagano}}, Nagano Prefecture, in the Kawagishi Higashi Hashiwara district (formerly {{ill|Kawagishi Village|ja|川岸村|橋原村|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawagishi Village|1=Kawagishi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q17175716}}). The shrine's former classification under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|en|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qid=Q712534|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was that of a {{ill|Village shrine (Japan)|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|qid=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine (Japan)|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q712534}}.
== Overview == The shrine enshrines {{ill|Moreya|en|Moreya|qid=Q11146526|lt=Moriya no Kami|1=Moreya}}, a deity from {{ill|Suwa|ja|諏訪地方|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 諏訪地域|1=Suwa Region|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11631849}} traditions. Its exact date of establishment is unknown. The shrine currently stands approximately 200 meters from the {{ill|Tenryū River|en|Tenryū River|qid=Q13667|lt=Tenryū River|1=Tenryū River}}, though it was originally said to be located by the riverbank.
Archaeological evidence suggests that the area surrounding the shrine has been inhabited since the {{ill|Jōmon period|en|Jōmon period|qid=Q52824|lt=Jōmon period|1=Jōmon period}}. Near the shrine's southwestern side lies the "Kyōzuka (Kyōzukahara) Site" <ref>{{Cite book|和書|year=1980|title=長野県中央道埋蔵文化財包蔵地発掘調査報告書 岡谷市その4 昭和52・53rd year 度|editor=長野県中央道遺跡調査団|publisher=[[Japan Highway Public Corporation]] Nagoya Construction Bureau, [[Nagano Prefectural Board of Education]]|chapter=第2節 経塚（経塚原）遺跡|pages=55-96|url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/en/8040}}</ref>, once believed to be the dwelling of {{ill|Moreya|en|Moreya|qid=Q11146526|lt=Moriya no Kami|1=Moreya}}.<ref>{{Cite book|和書|year=1924|title=諏訪史 第1巻|author=Torii Ryūzō|authorlink=Torii Ryūzō|publisher=Shinano Educational Society Suwa Branch|pages=373-374|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/935820/373}}</ref>
== deity == * '''{{ill|Moreya|en|Moreya|qid=Q11146526|lt=Moriya no Kami|1=Moreya}}''' : The local {{ill|Ubusunagami|en|Ubusunagami|qid=Q1392097|lt=Ubusunagami|1=Ubusunagami}} of the former Hashiwara Village and a distant ancestor of the {{ill|Moriya clan|ja|守矢氏|1=Moriya clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya clan|qq=}}, historically associated with the {{ill|Suwa Taisha Upper Shrine|ja|諏訪大社#上社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Taisha Upper Shrine|1=Suwa-taisha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q218813}}.
== History == According to the {{ill|Takeminakata#Mythological entry into Suwa|ja|タケミナカタ#明神入諏|Suwa Myths|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeminakata#Mythological entry into Suwa|qq=}}, Moriya no Kami, an indigenous {{ill|Kunitsukami|ja|国津神|1=Kunitsukami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsukami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天津神・国津神|comment=confirming this goes to Kunitsukami|1=Kunitsukami|2=zh|3=國神|4=fr|5=Kunitsukami|6=mni|7=ꯀꯨꯅꯤꯠꯁꯨꯀꯃꯤ}}, opposed the advance of {{ill|Takeminakata|en|Takeminakata|qid=Q9150391|qid=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata|1=Takeminakata}} (the Suwa deity) into Suwa. Following a battle, Moriya no Kami was defeated and submitted to Takeminakata, becoming his vassal and the progenitor of the {{ill|Moriya clan|ja|守矢氏|1=Moriya clan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya clan|13=User:Immanuelle/Moriya clan|qq=}}, who served the Suwa Upper Shrine.<ref name="Miyasaka">Miyasaka Mitsuaki, ''Suwa Taisha no Onbashira to Nenchū Gyōji'' (郷土出版社, 1992), pp. 91-93.</ref><ref name="moriyajinja">{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.nagano-jinjacho.jp/shibu/04nansin/02suwa/4030.html|title=Moriya Shrine|website=Nagano Shrine Association|accessdate=2018-10-01}}</ref>
Medieval texts locate the confrontation between the two deities near {{ill|Mount Moriya|ja|守屋山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Moriya|qq=}}, around the {{ill|Upper Shrine Main Sanctuary|ja|諏訪大社#上社|本宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Upper Shrine Main Sanctuary|1=Suwa-taisha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q218813}} area. However, later Edo-period traditions identify the {{ill|Tenryū River|en|Tenryū River|qid=Q13667|lt=Tenryū River|1=Tenryū River}} banks as their battleground. Today, Moriya Shrine and nearby {{ill|Fujishima Shrine (Suwa)|ja|藤島神社 (諏訪地方)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujishima Shrine (Suwa)|1=Fujishima Shrine (Suwa Region)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q56883936}} are recognized as the sites of their respective camps.<ref name="imai1">{{Cite book|和書|author=Imai Nokiku|year=1960|title=Suwa Monogatari|publisher=Kōyō Shobō|pages=3-15|chapter=Moriya no Kami and Takeminakata}}</ref><ref name="imai2">{{Cite book|和書|author=Imai Nokiku|year=1976|title=The Land of the Gods: Ancient Suwa Tales|publisher=Kokusho Kankōkai|pages=41-43}}</ref><ref name="moriya">{{Cite book|和書|author=Moriya Sanae|year=2017|title=The Guide to the Chino Moriya Archive|edition=Third Edition|pages=2-3|editor=Chino Moriya Archive|editor-link=神長官守矢史料館}}</ref>
It is said that the shrine originally stood in Hanoka Village (now Okaya's Minato district). When residents moved to Hashiwara Village, they brought Moriya Shrine with them.<ref name="hanaoka">{{Cite book|和書|editor=Hanoka District History Committee|year=1983|title=Hanoka District History|chapter=民間信仰と伝承|pages=620,637}}</ref>
=== The Fuji of the Tenryū River === A legend recounts that Moriya Shrine was once located by the riverbank. Wisteria trees in its precincts grew intertwined with those across the river at Fujishima Shrine, creating a bridge-like canopy. During the Kanbun era (1661–1673), the {{ill|Suwa Domain|en|Suwa Domain|qid=Q3034531|lt=Suwa Domain|1=Suwa Domain}} lord ordered their removal for a firefly-viewing event. However, local villagers feared divine retribution and refused. A man named Koishi Kaemon took on the task for a fee but soon fell into madness and met a tragic end, further cementing the wisteria’s sacred status.<ref name="imai2" /><ref name="moriyajinja" />
== Worship and Rituals == * [[Genshi-sai]] (元始祭) * {{ill|Insect-Sending Festival|ja|虫送り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Insect-Sending Festival|1=mushi okuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11241352}} (虫送り祭) * '''festival''' (festival): October 20 or the third Sunday in October * {{ill|Ninenmairi|en|Ninenmairi|qid=Q11371550|lt=Ninenmairi|1=Ninenmairi}} (二年参り): December 31 * '''Onbashira Festival''' (御柱祭): Conducted in years of the Tiger and Monkey : Historically aligned with the {{ill|Lower Shrine|ja|諏訪大社#下社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lower Shrine|1=Suwa-taisha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q218813}} festival but now held on separate dates.<ref name="moriyajinja" />
== Access == * 15 minutes (1.1 km) on foot from {{ill|Okaya Station|en|Okaya Station|qid=Q843826|lt=Okaya Station|1=Okaya Station}}, JR {{ill|Chūō Main Line|en|Chūō Main Line|qid=Q1078110|lt=Chūō Main Line|1=Chūō Main Line}} * 1 minute from "Moriya Shrine Front" bus stop, {{ill|Silky Bus Kawagishi Line|ja|シルキーバス川岸橋原線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Silky Bus Kawagishi Line|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
== Related Sites == === Former Moriya Ōkami Site === Located 300m from the shrine, marked by a stone monument with calligraphy by Moriya Jikken, the 75th shrine chief.<ref name="moriya" />
=== Ryūgen Mountain deity === A small shrine about 1.3 km from Moriya Shrine, dedicated to {{ill|Ōyama-tsumi|ja|大山祇神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyama-tsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ|1=Ōyamatsumi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q386563}}. The area was once part of Moriya no Kami’s sphere of influence.<ref name="hanaoka" />
